contentBlocks.original:
मङ्गलश्लोकाः
मङ्गलं भगवान्विष्णुर्मङ्गलं मधुसूदनः ।
मङ्गलं पुण्डरीकाक्षो मङ्गलं गरुडध्वजः ॥ 1
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṅgaḻaślōkāḥ
maṅgaḻaṃ bhagavānviṣṇurmaṅgaḻaṃ madhusūdanaḥ ।
maṅgaḻaṃ puṇḍarīkākṣō maṅgaḻaṃ garuḍadhvajaḥ ॥ 1
contentBlocks.translation: Maṅgalaślokas. Auspiciousness to Lord Vishnu; auspiciousness to Madhusudana (the slayer of the demon Madhu); auspiciousness to the lotus-eyed one; auspiciousness to the one whose banner bears the eagle Garuda. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
मङ्गलं कोसलेन्द्राय महनीयगुणाब्धये ।
चक्रवर्तितनूजाय सार्वभौमाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 2
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṅgaḻaṃ kōsalēndrāya mahanīyaguṇābdhayē ।
chakravartitanūjāya sārvabhaumāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 2
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the lord of Kosala, who is an ocean of exalted virtues; auspiciousness to the son of the emperor Dasharatha, the sovereign lord of all the earth. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
वेदवेदान्तवेद्याय मेघश्यामलमूर्तये ।
पुंसां मोहनरूपाय पुण्यश्लोकाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 3
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdavēdāntavēdyāya mēghaśyāmalamūrtayē ।
puṃsāṃ mōhanarūpāya puṇyaślōkāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 3
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who is known through the Vedas and Vedanta; to the one whose form is dark as a rain cloud; to the one whose beauty captivates all beings; auspiciousness to the one celebrated in sacred scripture. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वामित्रान्तरङ्गाय मिथिलानगरीपतेः ।
भाग्यानां परिपाकाय भव्यरूपाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 4
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvāmitrāntaraṅgāya mithilānagarīpatēḥ ।
bhāgyānāṃ paripākāya bhavyarūpāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 4
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who is dearest to the sage Vishvamitra; to the lord of the city of Mithila; auspiciousness to the one who is the fulfilment of all fortunes, of wondrous and beautiful form. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
पितृभक्ताय सततं भ्रातृभिः सह सीतया ।
नन्दिताखिललोकाय रामचन्द्राय मङ्गलम् ॥ 5
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pitṛbhaktāya satataṃ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha sītayā ।
nanditākhilalōkāya rāmachandrāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 5
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who is ever devoted to his father; who, together with his brothers and Sita, fills all the worlds with joy — auspiciousness to Ramachandra. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
त्यक्तसाकेतवासाय चित्रकूटविहारिणे ।
सेव्याय सर्वयमिनां धीरोदात्ताय मङ्गलम् ॥ 6
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tyaktasākētavāsāya chitrakūṭavihāriṇē ।
sēvyāya sarvayamināṃ dhīrōdāttāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 6
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who left the city of Ayodhya behind and dwelt in the forest of Chitrakuta; who is worthy of worship by all ascetics; auspiciousness to the noble and steadfast one. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
सौमित्रिणा च जानक्या चापबाणासिधारिणा ।
संसेव्याय सदा भक्त्या सानुजायास्तु मङ्गलम् ॥ 7
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saumitriṇā cha jānakyā chāpabāṇāsidhāriṇā ।
saṃsēvyāya sadā bhaktyā sānujāyāstu maṅgaḻam ॥ 7
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who is always lovingly served with devotion by Lakshmana and Janaki (Sita), and who bears the bow, arrows, and sword — auspiciousness to the one accompanied by his younger brother. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
दण्डकारण्यवासाय खण्डितामरशत्रवे ।
गृध्रराजाय भक्ताय मुक्तिदायास्तु मङ्गलम् ॥ 8
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daṇḍakāraṇyavāsāya khaṇḍitāmaraśatravē ।
gṛdhrarājāya bhaktāya muktidāyāstu maṅgaḻam ॥ 8
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who dwelt in the Dandaka forest and slew the enemies of the gods; auspiciousness to the one who is devoted to the king of vultures (Jatayu) and grants liberation. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
सादरं शबरीदत्तफलमूलाभिलाषिणे ।
सौलभ्यपरिपूर्णाय सत्त्वोद्युक्ताय मङ्गलम् ॥ 9
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sādaraṃ śabarīdattaphalamūlābhilāṣiṇē ।
saulabhyaparipūrṇāya sattvōdyuktāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 9
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who lovingly accepted the fruits and roots offered by Shabari with great reverence; who is full of approachable grace; auspiciousness to the one always united with virtue and vigour. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
हनूमत्समवेताय हरीशाभीष्टदायिने ।
वालिप्रमथनायास्तु महाधीराय मङ्गलम् ॥ 10
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hanūmatsamavētāya harīśābhīṣṭadāyinē ।
vālipramathanāyāstu mahādhīrāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 10
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one united with Hanuman; who fulfils the deepest wishes of the king of monkeys (Sugriva); auspiciousness to the one who subdued Vali, the supremely resolute hero. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमते रघुवीराय सेतुलङ्घितसिन्धवे ।
जितराक्षसराजाय रणधीराय मङ्गलम् ॥ 11
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmatē raghuvīrāya sētulaṅghitasindhavē ।
jitarākṣasarājāya raṇadhīrāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 11
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the glorious hero of the Raghu dynasty, who crossed the ocean by building the bridge (Setu) to Lanka; who vanquished the king of demons; auspiciousness to the steadfast warrior. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
आसाद्य नगरीं दिव्यामभिषिक्ताय सीतया ।
राजाधिराजराजाय रामभद्राय मङ्गलम् ॥ 12
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āsādya nagarīṃ divyāmabhiṣiktāya sītayā ।
rājādhirājarājāya rāmabhadrāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 12
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who reached the divine city and was consecrated together with Sita; to the king of kings, the sovereign of sovereigns — auspiciousness to the blessed Ramabhadra. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
विभीषणकृते प्रीत्या विश्वाभीष्टप्रदायिने ।
जानकीप्राणनाथाय सदा रामाय मङ्गलम् ॥ 13
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vibhīṣaṇakṛtē prītyā viśvābhīṣṭapradāyinē ।
jānakīprāṇanāthāya sadā rāmāya maṅgaḻam ॥ 13
contentBlocks.translation: Auspiciousness to the one who out of love for Vibhishana grants the wishes of all the worlds; to the lord of Janaki's life — auspiciousness always to Sri Rama. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामं त्रिजगद्गुरुं सुरवरं सीतामनोनायकं
श्यामाङ्गं शशिकोटिपूर्णवदनं चञ्चत्कलाकौस्तुभम् ।
सौम्यं सत्यगुणोत्तमं सुसरयूतीरे वसन्तं प्रभुं
त्रातारं सकलार्थसिद्धिसहितं वन्दे रघूणां पतिम् ॥ 14
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmaṃ trijagadguruṃ suravaraṃ sītāmanōnāyakaṃ
śyāmāṅgaṃ śaśikōṭipūrṇavadanaṃ chañchatkalākaustubham ।
saumyaṃ satyaguṇōttamaṃ susarayūtīrē vasantaṃ prabhuṃ
trātāraṃ sakalārthasiddhisahitaṃ vandē raghūṇāṃ patim ॥ 14
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sri Rama, the guru of the three worlds, the best of the gods, the beloved lord of Sita's heart — dark-limbed, with a face as full as the brightness of a crore of moons, bearing the gleaming Kaustubha gem, of serene and supremely virtuous nature, dwelling on the auspicious bank of the Sarayu river, the master who bestows the accomplishment of all ends; I bow to the lord of the Raghus. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीराघवं दशरथात्मजमप्रमेयं
सीतापतिं रघुवरान्वयरत्नदीपम् ।
आजानुबाहुमरविन्ददलायताक्षं
रामं निशाचरविनाशकरं नमामि ॥ 15
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāghavaṃ daśarathātmajamapramēyaṃ
sītāpatiṃ raghuvarānvayaratnadīpam ।
ājānubāhumaravindadaḻāyatākṣaṃ
rāmaṃ niśācharavināśakaraṃ namāmi ॥ 15
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sri Raghava, the immeasurable son of Dasharatha, the husband of Sita, the jewel-lamp of the noble Raghu lineage, long-armed as the knees, with eyes wide as lotus petals — Rama, the destroyer of the night-wandering demons. (15)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामचन्द्र करुणाकर राघवेन्द्र
राजेन्द्रचन्द्र रघुवंशसमुद्रचन्द्र ।
सुग्रीवनेत्रयुगलोत्पल-पूर्णचन्द्र
सीतामनःकुमुदचन्द्र नमो नमस्ते ॥ 16
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmachandra karuṇākara rāghavēndra
rājēndrachandra raghuvaṃśasamudrachandra ।
sugrīvanētrayugaḻōtpala-pūrṇachandra
sītāmanaḥkumudachandra namō namastē ॥ 16
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to you, O Sri Ramachandra — the moon of the sun-dynasty, the moon of the ocean of the Raghu clan, the full moon that causes the blue-lotus eyes of Sugriva to bloom, the moon that gladdens the white-lily heart of Sita. (16)

contentBlocks.original:
सीतामनोमानसराजहंस
संसारसन्तापहर क्षमावन् ।
श्रीराम दैत्यान्तक शान्तरूप
श्रीतारकब्रह्म नमो नमस्ते ॥ 17
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sītāmanōmānasarājahaṃsa
saṃsārasantāpahara kṣamāvan ।
śrīrāma daityāntaka śāntarūpa
śrītārakabrahma namō namastē ॥ 17
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to you, O Sri Rama — the royal swan gliding on the lake of Sita's mind, remover of the fever of worldly existence, full of forbearance; the destroyer of the demons, of tranquil form, the Brahman that is the refuge of the sacred name Tara. (17)

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णो राघव वासुदेव नृहरे देवौघचूडामणे ।
संसारार्णवकर्णधारक हरे कृष्णाय तुभ्यं नमः ॥ 18
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇō rāghava vāsudēva nṛharē dēvaughachūḍāmaṇē ।
saṃsārārṇavakarṇadhāraka harē kṛṣṇāya tubhyaṃ namaḥ ॥ 18
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you, O Vishnu, O Raghava, O Vasudeva, O Narasimha, O crest-jewel of the hosts of gods, O Hari who is the helmsman across the ocean of worldly existence; salutations to you, O Krishna. (18)

contentBlocks.original:
सुग्रीवादिसमस्तवानरवरैस्संसेव्यमानं सदा ।
विश्वामित्रपराशरादिमुनिभिस्संस्तूयमानं भजे ॥ 19
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sugrīvādisamastavānaravaraissaṃsēvyamānaṃ sadā ।
viśvāmitraparāśarādimunibhissaṃstūyamānaṃ bhajē ॥ 19
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama, who is reverently served at all times by Sugriva and all the monkey chiefs, and who is praised by the sages Vishvamitra, Parashara, and others. (19)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं चन्दनशीतलं क्षितिसुतामोहाकरं श्रीकरं
वैदेहीनयनारविन्दमिहिरं सम्पूर्णचन्द्राननम् ।
राजानं करुणासमेतनयनं सीतामनोनन्दनं
सीतादर्पणचारुगण्डललितं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 20
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ chandanaśītalaṃ kṣitisutāmōhākaraṃ śrīkaraṃ
vaidēhīnayanāravindamihiraṃ sampūrṇachandrānanam ।
rājānaṃ karuṇāsamētanayanaṃ sītāmanōnandanaṃ
sītādarpaṇachārugaṇḍalalitaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 20
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to Sri Raghava — cool as sandalwood, captivating to Sita's heart, the sun that causes the lotus-eyes of Vaidehi to bloom, whose face is like the full moon, whose eyes are filled with compassion, the delight of Sita's heart, with beautiful cheeks that shine like Sita's mirror. (20)

contentBlocks.original:
जानाति राम तव नामरुचिं महेशो
जानाति गौतमसती चरणप्रभावम् ।
जानाति दोर्बलपराक्रममीशचापो
जानात्यमोघपटुबाणगतिं पयोधिः ॥ 21
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jānāti rāma tava nāmaruchiṃ mahēśō
jānāti gautamasatī charaṇaprabhāvam ।
jānāti dōrbalaparākramamīśachāpō
jānātyamōghapaṭubāṇagatiṃ payōdhiḥ ॥ 21
contentBlocks.translation: Shiva knows the sweetness of your name, O Rama; the wife of Gautama knows the power of your feet. The bow of Ishvara (Shiva) knows the strength of your arms; the ocean knows the speed and unerring flight of your arrows. (21)

contentBlocks.original:
माता रामो मत्पिता रामचन्द्रो
भ्राता रामो मत्सखा राघवेशः ।
सर्वस्वं मे रामचन्द्रो दायालु-
र्नान्यं दैवं नैव जाने न जाने ॥ 22
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātā rāmō matpitā rāmachandrō
bhrātā rāmō matsakhā rāghavēśaḥ ।
sarvasvaṃ mē rāmachandrō dāyāḻu-
rnānyaṃ daivaṃ naiva jānē na jānē ॥ 22
contentBlocks.translation: Rama is my mother; Ramachandra is my father; Rama is my brother; Raghavesha is my companion. Ramachandra, the compassionate one, is my everything — I know no other god, none at all. (22)

contentBlocks.original:
विमलकमलनेत्रं विस्फुरन्नीलगात्रं
तपनकुलपवित्रं दानवध्वन्तमित्रम् ।
भुवनशुभचरित्रं भूमिपुत्रीकलत्रं
दशरथवरपुत्रं नौमि रामाख्यमित्रम् ॥ 23
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vimalakamalanētraṃ visphurannīlagātraṃ
tapanakulapavitraṃ dānavadhvantamitram ।
bhuvanaśubhacharitraṃ bhūmiputrīkaḻatraṃ
daśarathavaraputraṃ naumi rāmākhyamitram ॥ 23
contentBlocks.translation: I praise Rama — of pure lotus eyes, of shining blue form, purifier of the solar dynasty, friend who dispels the darkness of demons, of beautiful character that blesses all the worlds, whose consort is the daughter of the earth; the excellent son of Dasharatha, the friend called Rama. (23)

contentBlocks.original:
मार्गे मार्गे शाखिनां रत्नवेदी
वेद्यां वेद्यां किन्नरीबृन्दगीतम् ।
गीते गीते मञ्जुलालापगोष्ठी
गोष्ठ्यां गोष्ठ्यां त्वत्कथा रामचन्द्र ॥ 24
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mārgē mārgē śākhināṃ ratnavēdī
vēdyāṃ vēdyāṃ kinnarībṛndagītam ।
gītē gītē mañjulālāpagōṣṭhī
gōṣṭhyāṃ gōṣṭhyāṃ tvatkathā rāmachandra ॥ 24
contentBlocks.translation: On every path, upon the gem-studded altars of flowering trees, kinnaras (celestial musicians) sing in melodious chorus; in every song, in every gathering of sweet discourse — O Ramachandra, your story is ever on their lips. (24)

contentBlocks.original:
वृक्षे वृक्षे वीक्षिताः पक्षिसङ्घाः
सङ्घे सङ्घे मञ्जुलामोदवाक्यम् ।
वाक्ये वाक्ये मञ्जुलालापगोष्ठी
गोष्ठ्यां गोष्ठ्यां त्वत्कथा रामचन्द्र ॥ 25
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛkṣē vṛkṣē vīkṣitāḥ pakṣisaṅghāḥ
saṅghē saṅghē mañjulāmōdavākyam ।
vākyē vākyē mañjulālāpagōṣṭhī
gōṣṭhyāṃ gōṣṭhyāṃ tvatkathā rāmachandra ॥ 25
contentBlocks.translation: On every tree, flocks of birds gaze forth; in every group, sweet fragrant words arise; in every gathering of sweet discourse and song — O Ramachandra, your story is ever present. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
दुरिततिमिरचन्द्रो दुष्टकञ्जातचन्द्रः
सुरकुवलयचन्द्रस्सूर्यवंशाब्धिचन्द्रः ।
स्वजननिवहचन्द्रश्शत्रुराजीवचन्द्रः
प्रणतकुमुदचन्द्रः पातु मां रामचन्द्रः ॥ 26
contentBlocks.transliteration:
duritatimirachandrō duṣṭakañjātachandraḥ
surakuvalayachandrassūryavaṃśābdhichandraḥ ।
svajananivahachandraśśatrurājīvachandraḥ
praṇatakumudachandraḥ pātu māṃ rāmachandraḥ ॥ 26
contentBlocks.translation: May Ramachandra protect me — who is the moon that dispels the darkness of sin, the moon that destroys the lotus of the wicked, the moon that gladdens the blue-lotus of the gods, the moon of the ocean of the solar dynasty, the moon that gladdens his own people, the moon that destroys the red-lotus of the enemies, the moon that causes the white-lily of the devoted to bloom. (26)

contentBlocks.original:
कल्याणदं कौशिकयज्ञपालं
कलानिधिं काञ्चनशैलधीरम् ।
कञ्जातनेत्रं करुणासमुद्रं
काकुत्स्थरामं कलयामि चित्ते ॥ 27
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaḻyāṇadaṃ kauśikayajñapālaṃ
kaḻānidhiṃ kāñchanaśailadhīram ।
kañjātanētraṃ karuṇāsamudraṃ
kākutstharāmaṃ kalayāmi chittē ॥ 27
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate in my heart Kakutstha-Rama — the giver of auspiciousness, the protector of Vishvamitra's sacrifice, the ocean of the arts, as steadfast as a golden mountain, with lotus eyes, an ocean of compassion. (27)

contentBlocks.original:
राजीवायतलोचनं रघुवरं नीलोत्पलश्यामलं
मन्दाराञ्चितमण्डपे सुललिते सौवर्णके पुष्पके ।
आस्थाने नवरत्नराजिखचिते सिंहासने संस्थितं
सीतालक्ष्मणलोकपालसहितं वन्दे मुनीन्द्रास्पदम् ॥ 28
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rājīvāyatalōchanaṃ raghuvaraṃ nīlōtpalaśyāmalaṃ
mandārāñchitamaṇḍapē sulalitē sauvarṇakē puṣpakē ।
āsthānē navaratnarājikhachitē siṃhāsanē saṃsthitaṃ
sītālakṣmaṇalōkapālasahitaṃ vandē munīndrāspadam ॥ 28
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sri Raghava — lotus-eyed, of the Raghu dynasty, dark blue as the blue lotus, seated in a lovely golden pavilion adorned with coral-trees, established on a lion-throne inlaid with rows of nine gems, together with Sita, Lakshmana, and the guardians of the directions; the abode of sages. (28)

contentBlocks.original:
ध्याये रामं सुधांशुं नतसकलभवारण्यतापप्रहारम् ।
श्यामं शान्तं सुरेन्द्रं सुरमुनिविनुतं कोटिसूर्यप्रकाशम् ।
सीतासौमित्रिसेव्यं सुरनरसुगमं दिव्यसिंहासनस्थम् ।
सायाह्ने रामचन्द्रं स्मितरुचिरमुखं सर्वदा मे प्रसन्नम् ॥ 29
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāyē rāmaṃ sudhāṃśuṃ natasakalabhavāraṇyatāpaprahāram ।
śyāmaṃ śāntaṃ surēndraṃ suramunivinutaṃ kōṭisūryaprakāśam ।
sītāsaumitrisēvyaṃ suranarasugamaṃ divyasiṃhāsanastham ।
sāyāhnē rāmachandraṃ smitaruchiramukhaṃ sarvadā mē prasannam ॥ 29
contentBlocks.translation: I meditate on Ramachandra — who is like a full moon, who destroys the fever of the whole forest of worldly bondage for those who bow; dark, tranquil, the lord of the gods, praised by gods and sages, radiant as a crore of suns, served by Sita and Lakshmana, accessible to gods and men, enthroned on a divine lion-seat — may Ramachandra, of smiling and beautiful countenance, be always gracious to me in the evening hours. (29)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्रनीलमणिसन्निभदेहं
वन्दनीयमसकृन्मुनिबृन्दैः ।
लम्बमानतुलसीवनमालं
चिन्तयामि सततं रघुवीरम् ॥ 30
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indranīlamaṇisannibhadēhaṃ
vandanīyamasakṛnmunibṛndaiḥ ।
lambamānatulasīvanamālaṃ
chintayāmi satataṃ raghuvīram ॥ 30
contentBlocks.translation: I ceaselessly contemplate Raghuvira — whose body resembles the blue indranila gem, who is worthy of worship again and again by the hosts of sages, wearing a long garland of tulasi leaves. (30)

contentBlocks.original:
सम्पूर्णचन्द्रवदनं सरसीरुहाक्षं
माणिक्यकुण्डलधरं मुकुटाभिरामम् ।
चाम्पेयगौरवसनं शरचापहस्तं
श्रीरामचन्द्रमनिशं मनसा स्मरामि ॥ 31
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sampūrṇachandravadanaṃ sarasīruhākṣaṃ
māṇikyakuṇḍaladharaṃ mukuṭābhirāmam ।
chāmpēyagauravasanaṃ śarachāpahastaṃ
śrīrāmachandramaniśaṃ manasā smarāmi ॥ 31
contentBlocks.translation: I remember Sri Ramachandra unceasingly in my heart — whose face is like the full moon, whose eyes are like lotuses, who wears jewelled earrings, whose crown is supremely beautiful, dressed in golden-yellow garments, holding a bow in hand. (31)

contentBlocks.original:
मातुः पार्श्वे चरन्तं मणिमयशयने मञ्जुभूषाञ्चिताङ्गम् ।
मन्दं मन्दं पिबन्तं मुकुलितनयनं स्तन्यमन्यस्तनाग्रम् ।
अङ्गुल्याग्रैः स्पृशन्तं सुखपरवशया सस्मितालिङ्गिताङ्गम् ।
गाढं गाढं जनन्या कलयतु हृदयं मामकं रामबालम् ॥ 32
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātuḥ pārśvē charantaṃ maṇimayaśayanē mañjubhūṣāñchitāṅgam ।
mandaṃ mandaṃ pibantaṃ mukuḻitanayanaṃ stanyamanyastanāgram ।
aṅguḻyāgraiḥ spṛśantaṃ sukhaparavaśayā sasmitāliṅgitāṅgam ।
gāḍhaṃ gāḍhaṃ jananyā kalayatu hṛdayaṃ māmakaṃ rāmabālam ॥ 32
contentBlocks.translation: May my heart contemplate infant Rama — walking at his mother's side on a jewelled bed, his limbs adorned with lovely ornaments, drinking milk slowly with half-closed eyes, touching gently with his fingertips, embraced joyfully by his mother with a smile; held close and close again in his mother's arms. (32)

contentBlocks.original:
रामाभिरामं नयनाभिरामं
वाचाभिरामं वदनाभिरामम् ।
सर्वाभिरामं च सदाभिरामं
वन्दे सदा दाशरथिं च रामम् ॥ 33
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmābhirāmaṃ nayanābhirāmaṃ
vāchābhirāmaṃ vadanābhirāmam ।
sarvābhirāmaṃ cha sadābhirāmaṃ
vandē sadā dāśarathiṃ cha rāmam ॥ 33
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to Rama, the son of Dasharatha — charming to Rama's devotees, charming to the eyes, charming to the ear through his speech, charming in his face, charming in every way, and ever charming. (33)

contentBlocks.original:
राशब्दोच्चारमात्रेण मुखान्निर्याति पातकाः ।
पुनः प्रवेशभीत्या च मकारस्तु कवाटवत् ॥ 34
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāśabdōchchāramātrēṇa mukhānniryāti pātakāḥ ।
punaḥ pravēśabhītyā cha makārastu kavāṭavat ॥ 34
contentBlocks.translation: By merely uttering the syllable "Ra," all sins pour out from the mouth; and the syllable "Ma" acts like a closed door, so that they cannot re-enter out of fear. (34)

contentBlocks.original:
अनर्घमाणिक्यविराजमान-
श्रीपादुकालङ्कृतशोभनाभ्याम् ।
अशेषबृन्दारकवन्दिताभ्यां
नमो नमो रामपदाम्बुजाभ्याम् ॥ 35
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anarghamāṇikyavirājamāna-
śrīpādukālaṅkṛtaśōbhanābhyām ।
aśēṣabṛndārakavanditābhyāṃ
namō namō rāmapadāmbujābhyām ॥ 35
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to the lotus-feet of Rama — adorned with priceless ruby-encrusted sandals of great beauty, worshipped by all the hosts of immortals. (35)

contentBlocks.original:
चलत्कनककुण्डलोल्लसितदिव्यगण्डस्थलं
चराचरजगन्मयं चरणपद्मगङ्गाश्रयम् ।
चतुर्विधफलप्रदं चरमपीठमध्यस्थितं
चिदंशमखिलास्पदं दशरथात्मजं चिन्तये ॥ 36
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chalatkanakakuṇḍalōllasitadivyagaṇḍasthalaṃ
charācharajaganmayaṃ charaṇapadmagaṅgāśrayam ।
chaturvidhaphalapradaṃ charamapīṭhamadhyasthitaṃ
chidaṃśamakhilāspadaṃ daśarathātmajaṃ chintayē ॥ 36
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate the son of Dasharatha — whose divine cheeks glow with swinging golden earrings, who pervades the moving and unmoving universe, who is the refuge of the lotus-feet-born Ganga, who bestows the fourfold fruit of human life, who is established on the highest seat, who is the pure fragment of consciousness, the abode of all. (36)

contentBlocks.original:
सनन्दनमुनिप्रियं सकलवर्णवेदात्मकं
समस्तनिगमागमस्फुरिततत्त्वसिंहासनम् ।
सहस्रनयनाब्जजाद्यमरबृन्दसंसेवितं
समष्टिपुरवल्लभं दशरथात्मजं चिन्तये ॥ 37
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sanandanamunipriyaṃ sakalavarṇavēdātmakaṃ
samastanigamāgamasphuritatattvasiṃhāsanam ।
sahasranayanābjajādyamarabṛndasaṃsēvitaṃ
samaṣṭipuravallabhaṃ daśarathātmajaṃ chintayē ॥ 37
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate the son of Dasharatha — beloved of Sanandana and the great sages, who is the Self of all the Vedas in their many forms, whose lion-throne is the truth that shines through all scriptures, who is served by the hosts of immortals led by Brahma (the thousand-eyed), the lord of the entire universe. (37)

contentBlocks.original:
जाग्रत्स्वप्नसुषुप्ति-कालविलसत्तत्त्वात्मचिन्मात्रकं
चैतन्यात्मकमाधिपापरहितं भूम्यादितन्मात्रकम् ।
शाम्भव्यादिसमस्तयोगकुलकं साङ्ख्यादितत्त्वात्परं
शब्दावाच्यमहं नमामि सततं व्युत्पत्तिनाशात्परम् ॥ 38
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jāgratsvapnasuṣupti-kālavilasattattvātmachinmātrakaṃ
chaitanyātmakamādhipāparahitaṃ bhūmyāditanmātrakam ।
śāmbhavyādisamastayōgakulakaṃ sāṅkhyāditattvātparaṃ
śabdāvāchyamahaṃ namāmi satataṃ vyutpattināśātparam ॥ 38
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to that which transcends conceptual understanding — the pure consciousness that shines in the waking, dreaming, and deep-sleep states, the essence free from all sin, the origin of the subtle elements, the matrix of all yogic paths such as those of Shambhavi and others, beyond Sankhya and all philosophies, beyond what can be indicated by the word "I," beyond what is destroyed by realisation. (38)

contentBlocks.original:
इक्ष्वाकुवंशार्णवजातरत्नं
सीताङ्गनायौवनभाग्यरत्नम् ।
वैकुण्ठरत्नं मम भाग्यरत्नं
श्रीरामरत्नं शिरसा नमामि ॥ 39
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ikṣvākuvaṃśārṇavajātaratnaṃ
sītāṅganāyauvanabhāgyaratnam ।
vaikuṇṭharatnaṃ mama bhāgyaratnaṃ
śrīrāmaratnaṃ śirasā namāmi ॥ 39
contentBlocks.translation: I bow with my head to Sri Rama the Jewel — the jewel born from the ocean of the Ikshvaku dynasty, the jewel that is the fortune of Sita's youth, the jewel of Vaikuntha, the jewel that is my own good fortune. (39)

contentBlocks.original:
इक्ष्वाकुनन्दनं सुग्रीवपूजितं
त्रैलोक्यरक्षकं सत्यसन्धं सदा ।
राघवं रघुपतिं राजीवलोचनं
रामचन्द्रं भजे राघवेशं भजे ॥ 40
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ikṣvākunandanaṃ sugrīvapūjitaṃ
trailōkyarakṣakaṃ satyasandhaṃ sadā ।
rāghavaṃ raghupatiṃ rājīvalōchanaṃ
rāmachandraṃ bhajē rāghavēśaṃ bhajē ॥ 40
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra, the lord of the Raghus — the beloved of the Ikshvaku line, worshipped by Sugriva, protector of all three worlds, ever true to his word, Raghava, the lord of the Raghus, lotus-eyed. (40)

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तप्रियं भक्तसमाधिगम्यं
चिन्ताहरं चिन्तितकामधेनुम् ।
सूर्येन्दुकोटिद्युतिभास्वरं तं
रामं भजे राघवरामचन्द्रम् ॥ 41
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktapriyaṃ bhaktasamādhigamyaṃ
chintāharaṃ chintitakāmadhēnum ।
sūryēndukōṭidyutibhāsvaraṃ taṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghavarāmachandram ॥ 41
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the dear one of his devotees, who is attained through devotion, who removes anxiety and is the wish-fulfilling cow of all desires, who shines with the radiance of a crore of suns and moons — the Raghava Ramachandra. (41)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामं जनकक्षितीश्वरसुतावक्त्राम्बुजाहारिणं
श्रीमद्भानुकुलाब्धिकौस्तुभमणिं श्रीरत्नवक्षस्स्थलम् ।
श्रीकण्ठाद्यमरौघरत्नमकुटालङ्कारपादाम्बुजं
श्रीवत्सोज्ज्वलमिन्द्रनीलसदृशं श्रीरामचन्द्रं भजे ॥ 42
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmaṃ janakakṣitīśvarasutāvaktrāmbujāhāriṇaṃ
śrīmadbhānukulābdhikaustubhamaṇiṃ śrīratnavakṣassthalam ।
śrīkaṇṭhādyamaraugharatnamakuṭālaṅkārapādāmbujaṃ
śrīvatsōjjvalamindranīlasadṛśaṃ śrīrāmachandraṃ bhajē ॥ 42
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Ramachandra — who captivates the lotus-face of Sita, the daughter of Janaka, the Kaustubha gem of the glorious solar-dynasty ocean, with Sri on his broad chest, whose lotus feet are adorned by the crowns of Shiva and the hosts of gods, resplendent with the Shrivatsa mark, dark as the blue sapphire. (42)

contentBlocks.original:
रामचन्द्र चरिताकथामृतं
लक्ष्मणाग्रजगुणानुकीर्तनम् ।
राघवेश तव पादसेवनं
सम्भवन्तु मम जन्मजन्मनि ॥ 43
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmachandra charitākathāmṛtaṃ
lakṣmaṇāgrajaguṇānukīrtanam ।
rāghavēśa tava pādasēvanaṃ
sambhavantu mama janmajanmani ॥ 43
contentBlocks.translation: May the nectar of the deeds of Ramachandra, the singing of the virtues of the elder brother of Lakshmana, and the service of your feet, O lord of the Raghus — be mine birth after birth. (43)

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञानसम्भव-भवाम्बुधिबाडबाग्नि-
रव्यक्ततत्त्वनिकरप्रणवाधिरूढः ।
सीतासमेतमनुजेन हृदन्तराले
प्राणप्रयाणसमये मम सन्निधत्ते ॥ 44
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñānasambhava-bhavāmbudhibāḍabāgni-
ravyaktatattvanikarapraṇavādhirūḍhaḥ ।
sītāsamētamanujēna hṛdantarāḻē
prāṇaprayāṇasamayē mama sannidhattē ॥ 44
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Rama, who has taken the form of a man together with Sita, the fire that consumes the ocean of worldly existence born of ignorance, who is mounted upon the Pranava, the secret essence of the manifest tattvas — may he be present within my heart at the hour of death. (44)

contentBlocks.original:
रामो मत्कुलदैवतं सकरुणं रामं भजे सादरं
रामेणाखिलघोरपापनिहती रामाय तस्मै नमः ।
रामान्नास्ति जगत्रयैकसुलभो रामस्य दासोऽस्म्यहं
रामे प्रीतिरतीव मे कुलगुरो श्रीराम रक्षस्व माम् ॥ 45
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmō matkuladaivataṃ sakaruṇaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē sādaraṃ
rāmēṇākhilaghōrapāpanihatī rāmāya tasmai namaḥ ।
rāmānnāsti jagatrayaikasulabhō rāmasya dāsō'smyahaṃ
rāmē prītiratīva mē kulagurō śrīrāma rakṣasva mām ॥ 45
contentBlocks.translation: Rama is the deity of my family; I lovingly worship Rama with reverence. All grievous sins are destroyed by Rama; salutations to that Rama. There is none in the three worlds so easy to approach as Rama; I am his servant. My love for Rama is boundless — O Sri Rama, O ancestral guru, protect me. (45)

contentBlocks.original:
वैदेहीसहितं सुरद्रुमतले हैमे महामण्टपे ।
मध्येपुष्पकमासने मणिमये वीरासने संस्थितम् ।
अग्रे वाचयति प्रभञ्जनसुते तत्त्वं मुनिभ्यः परम् ।
व्याख्यान्तं भरतादिभिः परिवृतं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 46
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaidēhīsahitaṃ suradrumatalē haimē mahāmaṇṭapē ।
madhyēpuṣpakamāsanē maṇimayē vīrāsanē saṃsthitam ।
agrē vāchayati prabhañjanasutē tattvaṃ munibhyaḥ param ।
vyākhyāntaṃ bharatādibhiḥ parivṛtaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 46
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the dark-hued Rama — who, together with Vaidehi, is seated on a jewelled throne in a golden pavilion beneath a divine wish-fulfilling tree, in the midst of which Hanuman recites the supreme truth to the sages; who is surrounded by Bharata and the others. (46)

contentBlocks.original:
वामे भूमिसुता पुरस्तु हनुमान्पश्चात्सुमित्रासुत-
श्शत्रुघ्नो भरतश्च पार्श्वदलयोर्वाय्वादिकोणेष्वपि ।
सुग्रीवश्च विभीषणश्च युवराट् तारासुतो जाम्बवान्
मध्ये नीलसरोजकोमलरुचिं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 47
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāmē bhūmisutā purastu hanumānpaśchātsumitrāsuta-
śśatrughnō bharataścha pārśvadaḻayōrvāyvādikōṇēṣvapi ।
sugrīvaścha vibhīṣaṇaścha yuvarāṭ tārāsutō jāmbavān
madhyē nīlasarōjakōmalaruchiṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 47
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the dark-hued Rama — with Sita on his left, Hanuman in front, Lakshmana behind, Shatrughna and Bharata on either side, Sugriva, Vibhishana, the crown prince (Angada), Jambavan, and Vayu's attendants in all directions — gentle-glowing as a dark blue lotus in full bloom. (47)

contentBlocks.original:
केयूराङ्गदकङ्कणैर्मणिगणैर्वैरोचमानं सदा
राकापर्वणिचन्द्रकोटिसदृशं छत्रेण वैराजितम् ।
हेमस्तम्भसहस्रषोडशयुते मध्ये महामण्डपे
देवेशं भरतादिभिः परिवृतं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 48
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kēyūrāṅgadakaṅkaṇairmaṇigaṇairvairōchamānaṃ sadā
rākāparvaṇichandrakōṭisadṛśaṃ Chatrēṇa vairājitam ।
hēmastambhasahasraṣōḍaśayutē madhyē mahāmaṇḍapē
dēvēśaṃ bharatādibhiḥ parivṛtaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 48
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the dark-hued Rama — ever resplendent with armlets, bracelets, and gem-studded ornaments, like the full moon of the full-moon night, honoured with a royal parasol, seated in a great hall with sixteen thousand golden pillars, surrounded by Bharata and the others. (48)

contentBlocks.original:
साकेते शरदिन्दुकुन्दधवले सौघे महामण्टपे ।
पर्यस्तागरुधूपधूमपटले कर्पूरदीपोज्ज्वले ।
सुग्रीवाङ्गदवायुपुत्रसहितं सौमित्रिणा सेवितं
लीलामानुषविग्रहं रघुपतिं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 49
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākētē śaradindukundadhavaḻē saughē mahāmaṇṭapē ।
paryastāgarudhūpadhūmapaṭalē karpūradīpōjjvalē ।
sugrīvāṅgadavāyuputrasahitaṃ saumitriṇā sēvitaṃ
līlāmānuṣavigrahaṃ raghupatiṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 49
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghupati, the dark-hued Rama — in the great hall of Ayodhya, white as the autumn moon, filled with clouds of fragrant aguru incense, blazing with camphor lamps, served by Lakshmana, attended by Sugriva, Angada, and the son of the Wind; who has assumed a graceful human form. (49)

contentBlocks.original:
शान्तं शारदचन्द्रकोटिसदृशं चन्द्राभिरामाननं
चन्द्रार्काग्निविकासिकुण्डलधरं चन्द्रावतंसस्तुतम् ।
वीणापुस्तकसाक्षसूत्रविलसद्व्याख्यानमुद्राकरं
देवेशं भरतादिभिः परिवृतं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 50
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntaṃ śāradachandrakōṭisadṛśaṃ chandrābhirāmānanaṃ
chandrārkāgnivikāsikuṇḍaladharaṃ chandrāvataṃsastutam ।
vīṇāpustakasākṣasūtravilasadvyākhyānamudrākaraṃ
dēvēśaṃ bharatādibhiḥ parivṛtaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 50
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the dark-hued Rama — tranquil, like a crore of autumn moons, his face radiant as the moon, wearing earrings that contain the sun, moon, and fire, praised by those who wear the crescent moon (Shiva's devotees), with the vina and book, holding the commentary gesture, surrounded by Bharata and the others. (50)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं राक्षसमर्दनं रघुपतिं शक्रारिविध्वंसिनं
सुग्रीवेप्सितराज्यदं सुरपतेः पुत्रान्तकं शार्‍ङ्गिणम् ।
भक्तानामभयप्रदं भयहरं पापौघविध्वंसिनं
सीतासेवितपादपद्मयुगलं रामं भजे श्यामलम् ॥ 51
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ rākṣasamardanaṃ raghupatiṃ śakrārividhvaṃsinaṃ
sugrīvēpsitarājyadaṃ surapatēḥ putrāntakaṃ śār‍ṅgiṇam ।
bhaktānāmabhayapradaṃ bhayaharaṃ pāpaughavidhvaṃsinaṃ
sītāsēvitapādapadmayugaḻaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē śyāmalam ॥ 51
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the dark-hued Rama — the destroyer of demons, the lord of the Raghus, who crushed the enemies of Indra, who granted Sugriva his longed-for kingdom, who ended the son of the lord of the gods (Indrajit), bearing the Sharnga bow, who grants fearlessness to his devotees, who destroys fear and wipes away multitudes of sin, whose lotus feet are served by Sita. (51)

contentBlocks.original:
कन्दर्पायुतकोटिकोटितुलितं कालाम्बुदश्यामलं
कम्बुग्रीवमुदारकौस्तुभधरं कर्णावतंसोत्पलम् ।
कस्तूरीतिलकोज्ज्वलं स्मितमुखं चिन्मुद्रयालङ्कृतं
सीतालक्ष्मणवायुपुत्रसहितं सिंहासनस्थं भजे ॥ 52
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kandarpāyutakōṭikōṭitulitaṃ kālāmbudaśyāmalaṃ
kambugrīvamudārakaustubhadharaṃ karṇāvataṃsōtpalam ।
kastūrītilakōjjvalaṃ smitamukhaṃ chinmudrayālaṅkṛtaṃ
sītālakṣmaṇavāyuputrasahitaṃ siṃhāsanasthaṃ bhajē ॥ 52
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama seated on the lion-throne together with Sita, Lakshmana, and the son of Vayu — equal in beauty to ten million Kamadevs, dark as a rain cloud, with a conch-shell neck, wearing the noble Kaustubha gem, with lotus-flowers at his ears, a sandalwood mark gleaming on his brow, of smiling countenance, adorned with the gesture of supreme knowledge. (52)

contentBlocks.original:
साकेते नवरत्नपङ्क्तिखचिते चित्रध्वजालङ्कृते
वासे स्वर्णमये दलाष्टललिते पद्मे विमानोत्तमे ।
आसीनं भरतादिसोदरजनैः शाखामृगैः किन्नरैः
दिक्पालैर्मुनिपुङ्गवैर्नृपगणैस्संसेव्यमानं भजे ॥ 53
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākētē navaratnapaṅktikhachitē chitradhvajālaṅkṛtē
vāsē svarṇamayē daḻāṣṭalalitē padmē vimānōttamē ।
āsīnaṃ bharatādisōdarajanaiḥ śākhāmṛgaiḥ kinnaraiḥ
dikpālairmunipuṅgavairnṛpagaṇaissaṃsēvyamānaṃ bhajē ॥ 53
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama — seated in the finest celestial palace of Ayodhya, inlaid with rows of nine gems and adorned with flags, on an eight-petalled golden lotus of the finest celestial vehicle, waited upon by Bharata and brothers, monkeys, kinnaras, guardians of the directions, the foremost sages, and hosts of kings. (53)

contentBlocks.original:
कस्तूरीघनसारकुङ्कुमलसच्छ्रीचन्दनालङ्कृतं
कन्दर्पाधिकसुन्दरं घननिभं काकुत्स्थवंशध्वजम् ।
कल्याणाम्भरवेष्टितं कमलया युक्तं कलावल्लभं
कल्याणाचलकार्मुकप्रियसखं कल्याणरामं भजे ॥ 54
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kastūrīghanasārakuṅkumalasachChrīchandanālaṅkṛtaṃ
kandarpādhikasundaraṃ ghananibhaṃ kākutsthavaṃśadhvajam ।
kaḻyāṇāmbharavēṣṭitaṃ kamalayā yuktaṃ kalāvallabhaṃ
kaḻyāṇāchalakārmukapriyasakhaṃ kaḻyāṇarāmaṃ bhajē ॥ 54
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the auspicious Rama — adorned with sandal paste, saffron, and holy fragrance, more handsome than ten million Kamadevs, dark as a rain cloud, the banner of the Kakutstha dynasty, clad in auspicious garments, accompanied by Lakshmi, dear to the artful ones, whose beloved is the bow of the auspicious mountain (Shiva's bow). (54)

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्तेर्मूलं मुनिवरहृदानन्दकन्दं मुकुन्दं
कूटस्थाख्यं सकलवरदं सर्वचैतन्यरूपम् ।
नादातीतं कमलनिलयं नादनादान्ततत्त्वं
नादातीतं प्रकृतिरहितं रामचन्द्रं भजेऽहम् ॥ 55
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktērmūlaṃ munivarahṛdānandakandaṃ mukundaṃ
kūṭasthākhyaṃ sakalavaradaṃ sarvachaitanyarūpam ।
nādātītaṃ kamalanilayaṃ nādanādāntatattvaṃ
nādātītaṃ prakṛtirahitaṃ rāmachandraṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 55
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra — the root of liberation, the bulb of bliss in the hearts of the best sages, Mukunda (giver of liberation), known as Kutastha (the immovable witness), the giver of all boons, the form of universal consciousness, beyond the transcendent sound (Nada), the dwelling of Kamala (Lakshmi), the truth that is the end of Nada and its culmination, beyond Prakriti. (55)

contentBlocks.original:
ताराकारं निखिलनिलयं तत्त्वमस्यादिलक्ष्यं
शब्दावाच्यं त्रिगुणरहितं व्योममङ्गुष्ठमात्रम् ।
निर्वाणाख्यं सगुणमगुणव्योमरन्ध्रान्तरस्थं
सौषुम्नान्तः प्रणवसहितं रामचन्द्रं भजेऽहम् ॥ 56
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tārākāraṃ nikhilanilayaṃ tattvamasyādilakṣyaṃ
śabdāvāchyaṃ triguṇarahitaṃ vyōmamaṅguṣṭhamātram ।
nirvāṇākhyaṃ saguṇamaguṇavyōmarandhrāntarasthaṃ
sauṣumnāntaḥ praṇavasahitaṃ rāmachandraṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 56
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra — whose form is the sacred syllable Tara (Om), the universal abode, the goal indicated by "tat tvam asi" and other Vedic pronouncements, beyond verbal designation, free from the three gunas, of the size of a thumb dwelling in the space of the sky, known as Nirvana, with attributes yet beyond attributes, dwelling within the aperture of the Sushumna, together with the Pranava. (56)

contentBlocks.original:
निजानन्दाकारं निगमतुरगाराधितपदं
परब्रह्मानन्दं परमपदगं पापहरणम् ।
कृपापारावारं परमपुरुषं पद्मनिलयं
भजे रामं श्यामं प्रकृतिरहितं निर्गुणमहम् ॥ 57
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nijānandākāraṃ nigamaturagārādhitapadaṃ
parabrahmānandaṃ paramapadagaṃ pāpaharaṇam ।
kṛpāpārāvāraṃ paramapuruṣaṃ padmanilayaṃ
bhajē rāmaṃ śyāmaṃ prakṛtirahitaṃ nirguṇamaham ॥ 57
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama the dark one — of the form of pure bliss, whose feet are adored by the Vedas as swift horses, the bliss of the supreme Brahman, attaining the supreme abode, the destroyer of sin, an ocean of compassion overflowing its shores, the supreme Person, the dwelling of Padma (Lakshmi), free from Prakriti, without qualities. (57)

contentBlocks.original:
साकेते नगरे समस्तमहिमाधारे जगन्मोहने
रत्नस्तम्भसहस्रमण्टपमहासिंहासने साम्बुजे ।
विश्वामित्रवसिष्ठगौतमशुकव्यासादिभिर्मौनिभिः
ध्येयं लक्ष्मणलोकपालसहितं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 58
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākētē nagarē samastamahimādhārē jaganmōhanē
ratnastambhasahasramaṇṭapamahāsiṃhāsanē sāmbujē ।
viśvāmitravasiṣṭhagautamaśukavyāsādibhirmaunibhiḥ
dhyēyaṃ lakṣmaṇalōkapālasahitaṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 58
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama — seated in Ayodhya, the city that is the support of all glory, that captivates the universe, in a great hall with a thousand jewelled pillars, on a lotus-throne of the highest excellence, worthy of contemplation by the sages Vishvamitra, Vasishtha, Gautama, Shuka, Vyasa, and others, attended by Lakshmana, the guardians of the world, together with Sita. (58)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं श्यामाभिरामं रविशशिनयनं कोटिसूर्यप्रकाशं
दिव्यं दिव्यास्त्रपाणिं शरमुखशरधिं चारुकोडण्डहस्तम् ।
कालं कालाग्निरुद्रं रिपुकुलदहनं विघ्नविच्छेददक्षं
भीमं भीमाट्टहासं सकलभयहरं रामचन्द्रं भजेऽहम् ॥ 59
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ śyāmābhirāmaṃ raviśaśinayanaṃ kōṭisūryaprakāśaṃ
divyaṃ divyāstrapāṇiṃ śaramukhaśaradhiṃ chārukōḍaṇḍahastam ।
kālaṃ kālāgnirudraṃ ripukuladahanaṃ vighnavichChēdadakṣaṃ
bhīmaṃ bhīmāṭṭahāsaṃ sakalabhayaharaṃ rāmachandraṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 59
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra — of beautiful dark form, with sun and moon as eyes, blazing like a crore of suns, divine, holding supreme weapons, a quiver of arrows, bearing a magnificent bow, who is Time itself, the fire of final dissolution for enemy hosts, expert at cutting through obstacles, fearsome, with a great and resounding laugh, who removes all fear. (59)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामं भुवनैकसुन्दरतनुं धाराधरश्यामलं
राजीवायतलोचनं रघुवरं राकेन्दुबिम्बाननम् ।
कोदण्डादिनिजायुधाश्रितभुजैर्भ्रान्तं विदेहात्मजा-
धीशं भक्तजनावनं रघुवरं श्रीरामचन्द्रं भजे ॥ 60
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmaṃ bhuvanaikasundaratanuṃ dhārādharaśyāmalaṃ
rājīvāyatalōchanaṃ raghuvaraṃ rākēndubimbānanam ।
kōdaṇḍādinijāyudhāśritabhujairbhrāntaṃ vidēhātmajā-
dhīśaṃ bhaktajanāvanaṃ raghuvaraṃ śrīrāmachandraṃ bhajē ॥ 60
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Ramachandra, the Raghava — whose body alone is the beauty of the universe, dark as a rain cloud, with wide lotus eyes, whose face is like the orb of the full moon, whose long arms bear the bow and other weapons, the lord of Vaidehi (Sita), the protector of his devotees. (60)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवत्साङ्कमुदारकौस्तुभलसत्पीताम्बरालङ्कृतं
नानारत्नविराजमानमकुटं नीलाम्बुदश्यामलम् ।
कस्तूरीघनसारचर्चिततनुं मन्दारमालाधरं
कन्दर्पायुतसुन्दरं रघुपतिं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 61
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvatsāṅkamudārakaustubhalasatpītāmbarālaṅkṛtaṃ
nānāratnavirājamānamakuṭaṃ nīlāmbudaśyāmalam ।
kastūrīghanasāracharchitatanuṃ mandāramālādharaṃ
kandarpāyutasundaraṃ raghupatiṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 61
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghupati together with Sita — bearing the Shrivatsa mark, wearing the noble Kaustubha gem, adorned in yellow silk, with a crown resplendent with many jewels, dark as a rain cloud, his body anointed with musk and heavy sandal paste, wearing a garland of coral-tree flowers, more handsome than ten million Kamadevs. (61)

contentBlocks.original:
सदानन्ददेवे सहस्रारपद्मे
गलच्चन्द्रपीयूषधारामृतान्ते ।
स्थितं राममूर्तिं निषेवे निषेवे-
ऽन्यदैवं न सेवे न सेवे न सेवे ॥ 62
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadānandadēvē sahasrārapadmē
galachchandrapīyūṣadhārāmṛtāntē ।
sthitaṃ rāmamūrtiṃ niṣēvē niṣēvē-
'nyadaivaṃ na sēvē na sēvē na sēvē ॥ 62
contentBlocks.translation: I worship, I worship the form of Rama established in the ever-blissful thousand-petalled lotus, at the end of the dripping stream of lunar nectar — no other deity do I serve, serve, serve. (62)

contentBlocks.original:
सुधाभासितद्वीपमध्ये विमाने
सुपर्वालिवृक्षोज्ज्वले शेषतल्पे ।
निषण्णं रमाङ्कं निषेवे निषेवे-
ऽन्यदैवं न सेवे न सेवे न सेवे ॥ 63
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhābhāsitadvīpamadhyē vimānē
suparvāḻivṛkṣōjjvalē śēṣatalpē ।
niṣaṇṇaṃ ramāṅkaṃ niṣēvē niṣēvē-
'nyadaivaṃ na sēvē na sēvē na sēvē ॥ 63
contentBlocks.translation: I worship, I worship Sri Rama reclining upon Lakshmi's lap on the serpent-couch in the celestial island washed by nectar, in a divine palace adorned by the heavenly wish-fulfilling tree — no other deity do I serve, serve, serve. (63)

contentBlocks.original:
चिदंशं समानन्दमानन्दकन्दं
सुषुम्नाख्यरन्ध्रान्तराले च हंसम् ।
सचक्रं सशङ्खं सपीताम्बराङ्कं
परञ्चान्यदैवं न जाने न जाने ॥ 64
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chidaṃśaṃ samānandamānandakandaṃ
suṣumnākhyarandhrāntarāḻē cha haṃsam ।
sachakraṃ saśaṅkhaṃ sapītāmbarāṅkaṃ
parañchānyadaivaṃ na jānē na jānē ॥ 64
contentBlocks.translation: I know of none other, none other — the fragment of consciousness, the bliss, the bulb of bliss that is the swan within the aperture of the Sushumna, bearing the discus and the conch, dressed in yellow, the supreme reality beyond all this. (64)

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्वेदकूटोल्लसत्कारणाख्यं
स्फुरद्दिव्यवैमानिके भोगितल्पे ।
परन्धाममूर्तिं निषण्णं निषेवे
निषेवेऽन्यदैवं न सेवे न सेवे ॥ 65
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturvēdakūṭōllasatkāraṇākhyaṃ
sphuraddivyavaimānikē bhōgitalpē ।
parandhāmamūrtiṃ niṣaṇṇaṃ niṣēvē
niṣēvē'nyadaivaṃ na sēvē na sēvē ॥ 65
contentBlocks.translation: I worship, I worship the form of the supreme abode, established in the luminous celestial vehicle, the causal reality that shines at the summit of the four Vedas, reclining on the serpent-couch — no other deity do I serve, serve. (65)

contentBlocks.original:
सिंहासनस्थं सुरसेवितव्यं
रत्नाङ्कितालङ्कृतपादपद्मम् ।
सीतासमेतं शशिसूर्यनेत्रं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 66
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siṃhāsanasthaṃ surasēvitavyaṃ
ratnāṅkitālaṅkṛtapādapadmam ।
sītāsamētaṃ śaśisūryanētraṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 66
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — enthroned on the lion-seat, worthy of adoration by the gods, whose lotus feet are inlaid with gems, attended by Sita, with the moon and sun as his two eyes. (66)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं पुराणपुरुषं रमणीयवेषं
राजाधिराजमकुटार्चितपादपीठम् ।
सीतापतिं सुनयनं जगदेकवीरं
श्रीरामचन्द्रमनिशं कलयामि चित्ते ॥ 67
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ purāṇapuruṣaṃ ramaṇīyavēṣaṃ
rājādhirājamakuṭārchitapādapīṭham ।
sītāpatiṃ sunayanaṃ jagadēkavīraṃ
śrīrāmachandramaniśaṃ kalayāmi chittē ॥ 67
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate in my heart Sri Ramachandra unceasingly — the ancient Person, of beautiful attire, whose footstool is worshipped by the crowns of kings of kings, the husband of Sita, of beautiful eyes, the sole hero of the universe. (67)

contentBlocks.original:
परानन्दवस्तुस्वरूपादिसाक्षिं
परब्रह्मगम्यं परञ्ज्योतिमूर्तिम् ।
पराशक्तिमित्राऽप्रियाराधिताङ्घ्रिं
परन्धामरूपं भजे रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 68
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parānandavastusvarūpādisākṣiṃ
parabrahmagamyaṃ parañjyōtimūrtim ।
parāśaktimitrā'priyārādhitāṅghriṃ
parandhāmarūpaṃ bhajē rāmachandram ॥ 68
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra — the witness that is the form of supreme bliss, the path to the supreme Brahman, the form of the supreme light, whose feet are worshipped by the supremely auspicious Parashat (Shakti), the form of the supreme abode. (68)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दस्मितं कुण्डलगण्डभागं
पीताम्बरं भूषणभूषिताङ्गम् ।
नीलोत्पलाङ्गं भुवनैकमित्रं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 69
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandasmitaṃ kuṇḍalagaṇḍabhāgaṃ
pītāmbaraṃ bhūṣaṇabhūṣitāṅgam ।
nīlōtpalāṅgaṃ bhuvanaikamitraṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 69
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — of gentle smile, with earrings adorning his beautiful cheeks, clad in yellow silk, with all limbs adorned with ornaments, dark as the blue lotus, the sole friend of all the worlds. (69)

contentBlocks.original:
अचिन्त्यमव्यक्तमनन्तरूप-
मद्वैतमानन्दमनादिगम्यम् ।
पुण्यस्वरूपं पुरुषोत्तमाख्यं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 70
contentBlocks.transliteration:
achintyamavyaktamanantarūpa-
madvaitamānandamanādigamyam ।
puṇyasvarūpaṃ puruṣōttamākhyaṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 70
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — inconceivable, unmanifest, of infinite forms, non-dual, the bliss, beginninglessly beyond reach, of the nature of pure merit, known as Purushottama (the highest Person). (70)

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मासनस्थं सुरसेवितव्यं
पद्मालयानन्दकटाक्षवीक्ष्यम् ।
गन्धर्वविद्याधरगीयमानं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 71
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmāsanasthaṃ surasēvitavyaṃ
padmālayānandakaṭākṣavīkṣyam ।
gandharvavidyādharagīyamānaṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 71
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — seated on the lotus-throne, worthy of adoration by the gods, gazed upon by the compassionate glance of Lakshmi (Padmalaya), sung of by Gandharvas and Vidyadharas. (71)

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तकीर्तिं वरदं प्रसन्नं
पद्मासनं सेवकपारिजातम् ।
राजाधिराजं रघुवीरकेतुं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 72
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantakīrtiṃ varadaṃ prasannaṃ
padmāsanaṃ sēvakapārijātam ।
rājādhirājaṃ raghuvīrakētuṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 72
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — of infinite renown, the giver of boons, the gracious one, seated on the lotus-throne, the wish-fulfilling tree for his servants, the king of kings, the banner of Raghuvira. (72)

contentBlocks.original:
सुग्रीवमित्रं सुजनानुरूपं
लङ्काहरं राक्षसवंशनाशम् ।
वेदाश्रयाङ्गं विपुलायताक्षं
रामं भजे राघव रामचन्द्रम् ॥ 73
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sugrīvamitraṃ sujanānurūpaṃ
laṅkāharaṃ rākṣasavaṃśanāśam ।
vēdāśrayāṅgaṃ vipulāyatākṣaṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghava rāmachandram ॥ 73
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — the friend of Sugriva, suited to noble souls, the destroyer of Lanka, the annihilator of the demon race, whose body is the very form of the Vedas, of wide and expansive eyes. (73)

contentBlocks.original:
सकृत्प्रणतरक्षायां साक्षी यस्य विभीषणः ।
सापराधप्रतीकारः स श्रीरामो गतिर्मम ॥ 74
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakṛtpraṇatarakṣāyāṃ sākṣī yasya vibhīṣaṇaḥ ।
sāparādhapratīkāraḥ sa śrīrāmō gatirmama ॥ 74
contentBlocks.translation: Vibhishana is the witness of his vow to protect those who bow to him even once — that Sri Rama who punishes the transgressor is my refuge and final goal. (74)

contentBlocks.original:
फलमूलाशिनौ दान्तौ तापसौ धर्मचारिणौ ।
रक्षःकुलविहन्तारौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ ॥ 75
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalamūlāśinau dāntau tāpasau dharmachāriṇau ।
rakṣaḥkulavihantārau bhrātarau rāmalakṣmaṇau ॥ 75
contentBlocks.translation: The two brothers Rama and Lakshmana — living on fruits and roots, self-restrained, practising asceticism and righteousness, destroyers of the demon race. (75)

contentBlocks.original:
तरुणौ रूपसम्पन्नौ सुकुमारौ महाबलौ ।
पुण्डरीक विशालाक्षौ चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरौ ॥ 76
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taruṇau rūpasampannau sukumārau mahābalau ।
puṇḍarīka viśālākṣau chīrakṛṣṇājināmbarau ॥ 76
contentBlocks.translation: Both young, endowed with beauty, tender and supremely strong, with eyes wide as white lotuses, clad in bark garments and black deerskin. (76)

contentBlocks.original:
कौसल्यानयनेन्दुं दशरथमुखारविन्दमार्ताण्डम् ।
सीतामानसहंसं रामं राजीवलोचनं वन्दे ॥ 77
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kausalyānayanēnduṃ daśarathamukhāravindamārtāṇḍam ।
sītāmānasahaṃsaṃ rāmaṃ rājīvalōchanaṃ vandē ॥ 77
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Rama, the moon of Kausalya's eyes, the sun to the lotus-face of Dasharatha, the royal swan on the lake of Sita's heart, the lotus-eyed one. (77)

contentBlocks.original:
भर्जनं भवबीजानां मार्जनं सुखसम्पदाम् ।
तर्जनं यमदूतानां रामरामेति कीर्तनम् ॥ 78
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bharjanaṃ bhavabījānāṃ mārjanaṃ sukhasampadām ।
tarjanaṃ yamadūtānāṃ rāmarāmēti kīrtanam ॥ 78
contentBlocks.translation: The chanting of "Rama, Rama" burns the seeds of worldly bondage, cleanses away the miseries of happiness's absence, and frightens away the messengers of Yama (Death). (78)

contentBlocks.original:
न जाने जानकी जाने राम त्वन्नामवैभवम् ।
सर्वेशो भगवान् शम्भुर्वाल्मीकिर्वेत्ति वा नवा ॥ 79
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na jānē jānakī jānē rāma tvannāmavaibhavam ।
sarvēśō bhagavān śambhurvālmīkirvētti vā navā ॥ 79
contentBlocks.translation: O Janaki (Sita) knows; yet even she does not fully know, O Rama, the greatness of your name. Does the lord of all, the blessed Shambhu (Shiva), know? Does Valmiki? Or does anyone at all? (79)

contentBlocks.original:
करतलधृतचापं कालमेघस्वरूपं
सरसिजदलनेत्रं चारुहासं सुगात्रम् ।
विचिनुतवनवासं विक्रमोदग्रवेषं
प्रणमत रघुनाथं जानकीप्राणनाथम् ॥ 80
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karataladhṛtachāpaṃ kālamēghasvarūpaṃ
sarasijadaḻanētraṃ chāruhāsaṃ sugātram ।
vichinutavanavāsaṃ vikramōdagravēṣaṃ
praṇamata raghunāthaṃ jānakīprāṇanātham ॥ 80
contentBlocks.translation: Bow to Raghunatha, the lord of Janaki's life — bearing a bow in his lotus hand, of the form of the black rain cloud, with eyes like petals of the lotus, of beautiful smile, of lovely limbs, roaming the forest in his wandering exile, of extraordinary heroic bearing. (80)

contentBlocks.original:
विद्युत्स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलदीप्तचारु-
गण्डस्थलं मणिकिरीटविराजमानम् ।
पीताम्बरं जलदनीलमुदारकान्तिं
श्रीरामचन्द्रमनिशं कलयामि चित्ते ॥ 81
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidyutsphuranmakarakuṇḍaladīptachāru-
gaṇḍasthalaṃ maṇikirīṭavirājamānam ।
pītāmbaraṃ jaladanīlamudārakāntiṃ
śrīrāmachandramaniśaṃ kalayāmi chittē ॥ 81
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate Sri Ramachandra unceasingly in my heart — whose beautiful cheeks glow with earrings shaped like makaras (sea-creatures) flashing like lightning, resplendent with a gem-studded crown, clad in yellow silk, of the dark beauty of a rain cloud, of generous and beautiful radiance. (81)

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नोल्लसज्ज्वलितकुण्डलगण्डभागं
कस्तूरिकातिलकशोभितफालभागम् ।
कर्णान्तदीर्घनयनं करुणाकटाक्षं
श्रीरामचन्द्र मुखमात्मनि सन्निधत्तम् ॥ 82
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnōllasajjvalitakuṇḍalagaṇḍabhāgaṃ
kastūrikātilakaśōbhitaphālabhāgam ।
karṇāntadīrghanayanaṃ karuṇākaṭākṣaṃ
śrīrāmachandra mukhamātmani sannidhattam ॥ 82
contentBlocks.translation: May the face of Sri Ramachandra be present within me — whose cheeks gleam with blazing jewelled earrings, whose broad forehead shines with a musk sandalwood tilaka mark, with eyes long as to the very ears, whose glance is full of compassion. (82)

contentBlocks.original:
वैदेहीसहितं च लक्ष्मणयुतं कैकेयिपुत्रान्वितं
सुग्रीवं च विभीषणानिलसुतौ नीलं नलं साङ्गदम् ।
विश्वामित्रवसिष्ठगौतमभरद्वाजादिकान् मानयन्
रामो मारुतिसेवितः स्मरतु मां साम्राज्यसिंहासने ॥ 83
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaidēhīsahitaṃ cha lakṣmaṇayutaṃ kaikēyiputrānvitaṃ
sugrīvaṃ cha vibhīṣaṇānilasutau nīlaṃ nalaṃ sāṅgadam ।
viśvāmitravasiṣṭhagautamabharadvājādikān mānayan
rāmō mārutisēvitaḥ smaratu māṃ sāmrājyasiṃhāsanē ॥ 83
contentBlocks.translation: May Rama, served by Hanuman, remember me from his imperial throne — attended by Vaidehi, Lakshmana, the sons of Kaikeyi (Bharata and Shatrughna), Sugriva, Vibhishana, the son of Vayu, Nila, Nala, and Angada, honouring Vishvamitra, Vasishtha, Gautama, Bharadvaja, and other sages. (83)

contentBlocks.original:
सकलगुणनिधानं योगिभिस्स्तूयमानं
भजितसुरविमानं रक्षितेन्द्रादिमानम् ।
महितवृषभयानं सीतया शोभमानं
स्मरतु हृदयभानुं ब्रह्मरामाभिरामम् ॥ 84
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalaguṇanidhānaṃ yōgibhisstūyamānaṃ
bhajitasuravimānaṃ rakṣitēndrādimānam ।
mahitavṛṣabhayānaṃ sītayā śōbhamānaṃ
smaratu hṛdayabhānuṃ brahmarāmābhirāmam ॥ 84
contentBlocks.translation: May the sun in the heart, the one beautiful as Brahman and Rama, who is the treasury of all virtues, praised by yogis, served by the celestial vehicles of the gods, whose reputation protects the dignity of Indra and others, who is revered as the great lord, who shines together with Sita, the ocean celebrated by many — may he remember me. (84)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिदशकुमुदचन्द्रो दानवाम्भोजचन्द्रो
दुरिततिमिरचन्द्रो योगिनां ज्ञानचन्द्रः ।
प्रणतनयनचन्द्रो मैथिलीनेत्रचन्द्रो
दशमुखरिपुचन्द्रः पातु मां रामचन्द्रः ॥ 85
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tridaśakumudachandrō dānavāmbhōjachandrō
duritatimirachandrō yōgināṃ jñānachandraḥ ।
praṇatanayanachandrō maithilīnētrachandrō
daśamukharipuchandraḥ pātu māṃ rāmachandraḥ ॥ 85
contentBlocks.translation: May Ramachandra protect me — the moon that gladdens the white-lily of the celestial beings, the moon that destroys the lotus of the demons, the moon that dispels the darkness of sin, the moon of wisdom for the yogis, the moon for the eyes of those who bow, the moon for the eyes of Maithili (Sita), the moon who is the enemy of the ten-faced one (Ravana). (85)

contentBlocks.original:
यन्नामैव सहस्रनामसदृशं यन्नाम वेदैस्समं
यन्नामाङ्कितवाक्य-मासुरबलस्त्रीगर्भविच्छेदनम् ।
यन्नाम श्वपचार्यभेदरहितं मुक्तिप्रदानोज्ज्वलं
तन्नामाऽलघुरामरामरमणं श्रीरामनामामृतम् ॥ 86
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yannāmaiva sahasranāmasadṛśaṃ yannāma vēdaissamaṃ
yannāmāṅkitavākya-māsurabalastrīgarbhavichChēdanam ।
yannāma śvapachāryabhēdarahitaṃ muktipradānōjjvalaṃ
tannāmā'laghurāmarāmaramaṇaṃ śrīrāmanāmāmṛtam ॥ 86
contentBlocks.translation: His very name is equal to a thousand names; his name is equal to the Vedas; a verse marked with his name destroys the womb of the wives of the demon armies. His name makes no distinction between the brahmin and the outcast, and shines as the bestower of liberation — that name, the Rama-Rama chant, is the heavy nectar of the name of Sri Rama. (86)

contentBlocks.original:
राजीवनेत्र रघुपुङ्गव रामभद्र
राकेन्दुबिम्बसदृशानन नीलगात्र ।
रामाऽभिराम रघुवंशसमुद्भव त्वं
श्रीरामचन्द्र मम देहि करावलम्बम् ॥ 87
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rājīvanētra raghupuṅgava rāmabhadra
rākēndubimbasadṛśānana nīlagātra ।
rāmā'bhirāma raghuvaṃśasamudbhava tvaṃ
śrīrāmachandra mama dēhi karāvalambam ॥ 87
contentBlocks.translation: O lotus-eyed one, O best of the Raghu dynasty, O Ramabhadra, whose face is like the orb of the full moon, O dark-limbed one, O beautiful Rama, born of the Raghu clan — O Sri Ramachandra, give me the support of your hand. (87)

contentBlocks.original:
माणिक्यमञ्जीरपदारविन्दं
रामार्कसम्फुल्लमुखारविन्दम् ।
भक्ताभयप्रापिकरारविन्दां
देवीं भजे राघववल्लभां ताम् ॥ 88
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māṇikyamañjīrapadāravindaṃ
rāmārkasamphullamukhāravindam ।
bhaktābhayaprāpikarāravindāṃ
dēvīṃ bhajē rāghavavallabhāṃ tām ॥ 88
contentBlocks.translation: I worship that Goddess, the beloved of Raghava (Sita) — whose lotus feet are adorned with jewelled anklets, whose lotus face is like the sun of Rama's delight, whose lotus hands grant fearlessness to the devoted. (88)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु विजयकारी जानकीमोदकारी
तपनकुलविहारी दण्डकारण्यचारी ।
दशवदनकुठारी दैत्यविच्छेदकारी
मणिमकुटकधारी चण्डकोदण्डधारी ॥ 89
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu vijayakārī jānakīmōdakārī
tapanakulavihārī daṇḍakāraṇyachārī ।
daśavadanakuṭhārī daityavichChēdakārī
maṇimakuṭakadhārī chaṇḍakōdaṇḍadhārī ॥ 89
contentBlocks.translation: Victory to the bringer of victory, the bringer of joy to Janaki, the one who roams in the solar dynasty, the wanderer of the Dandaka forest, the axe against the ten-headed one (Ravana), the scatterer of the demon armies, wearing a jewelled crown, bearing the fierce great bow. (89)

contentBlocks.original:
रामः पिता रघव एव माता
रामस्सुबन्धुश्च सखा हितश्च ।
रामो गुरुर्मे परमं च दैवं
रामं विना नाऽन्यमहं स्मरामि ॥ 90
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaḥ pitā raghava ēva mātā
rāmassubandhuścha sakhā hitaścha ।
rāmō gururmē paramaṃ cha daivaṃ
rāmaṃ vinā nā'nyamahaṃ smarāmi ॥ 90
contentBlocks.translation: Rama is my father; Raghava himself is my mother; Rama is my brother and my friend, the lord of the Raghus. Ramachandra, the compassionate, is my everything — beyond Rama I do not remember anyone. (90)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीराम मे त्वं हि पिता च माता
श्रीराम मे त्वं हि सुहृच्च बन्धुः ।
श्रीराम मे त्वं हि गुरुश्च गोष्ठी
श्रीराम मे त्वं हि समस्तमेव ॥ 91
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāma mē tvaṃ hi pitā cha mātā
śrīrāma mē tvaṃ hi suhṛchcha bandhuḥ ।
śrīrāma mē tvaṃ hi guruścha gōṣṭhī
śrīrāma mē tvaṃ hi samastamēva ॥ 91
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri Rama, you alone are my father and mother; O Sri Rama, you alone are my dear friend and kinsman; O Sri Rama, you alone are my guru and companion; O Sri Rama, you alone are my everything. (91)

contentBlocks.original:
रामचन्द्रचरितामृतपानं
सोमपानशतकोटिसमानम् ।
सोमपानशतकोटिभिरीया-
ज्जन्म नैति रघुनायकनाम्ना ॥ 92
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmachandracharitāmṛtapānaṃ
sōmapānaśatakōṭisamānam ।
sōmapānaśatakōṭibhirīyā-
jjanma naiti raghunāyakanāmnā ॥ 92
contentBlocks.translation: Drinking the nectar of the deeds of Ramachandra is equal to drinking soma a hundred crore times. One does not escape rebirth by drinking soma a hundred crore times — but through the name of Raghunayaka one does. (92)

contentBlocks.original:
राम राम दयासिन्धो रावणारे जगत्पते ।
त्वत्पादकमलासक्ति-र्भवेज्जन्मनि जन्मनि ॥ 93
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāma rāma dayāsindhō rāvaṇārē jagatpatē ।
tvatpādakamalāsakti-rbhavējjanmani janmani ॥ 93
contentBlocks.translation: O Rama, O Rama, ocean of compassion, O enemy of Ravana, O lord of the universe — may devotion to your lotus feet be mine birth after birth. (93)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामचन्द्रेति दयापरेति
भक्तप्रियेति भवबन्धनमोचनेति ।
नाथेति नागशयनेति सदा स्तुवन्तं
मां पाहि भीतमनिशं कृपणं कृपालो ॥ 94
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmachandrēti dayāparēti
bhaktapriyēti bhavabandhanamōchanēti ।
nāthēti nāgaśayanēti sadā stuvantaṃ
māṃ pāhi bhītamaniśaṃ kṛpaṇaṃ kṛpāḻō ॥ 94
contentBlocks.translation: O compassionate one, O lover of your devotees, O liberator from the bonds of worldly existence, O lord, O recliner on the serpent couch — protect me always as I praise you thus, wretched and afraid, O compassionate one. (94)

contentBlocks.original:
अयोध्यानाथ राजेन्द्र सीताकान्त जगत्पते ।
श्रीराम पुण्डरीकाक्ष रामचन्द्र नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 95
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayōdhyānātha rājēndra sītākānta jagatpatē ।
śrīrāma puṇḍarīkākṣa rāmachandra namō'stu tē ॥ 95
contentBlocks.translation: O lord of Ayodhya, O king of kings, O dear one of Sita, O lord of the universe, O Sri Rama, O lotus-eyed one, O Ramachandra — salutations to you. (95)

contentBlocks.original:
हे राम हे रमण हे जगदेकवीर
हे नाथ हे रघुपते करुणालवाल ।
हे जानकीरमण हे जगदेकबन्धो
मां पाहि दीनमनिशं कृपणं कृतघ्नम् ॥ 96
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hē rāma hē ramaṇa hē jagadēkavīra
hē nātha hē raghupatē karuṇālavāla ।
hē jānakīramaṇa hē jagadēkabandhō
māṃ pāhi dīnamaniśaṃ kṛpaṇaṃ kṛtaghnam ॥ 96
contentBlocks.translation: O Ramabhadra, my refuge, O compassionate one, O sole shelter of the devotees, O Janaki's beloved, O sole friend of the universe — protect me always, wretched and helpless, ungrateful that I am. (96)

contentBlocks.original:
जानाति राम तव तत्त्वगतिं हनूमान् ।
जानाति राम तव सख्यगतिं कपीशः ।
जानाति राम तव युद्धगतिं दशास्यो ।
जानाति राम धनदानुज एव सत्यम् ॥ 97
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jānāti rāma tava tattvagatiṃ hanūmān ।
jānāti rāma tava sakhyagatiṃ kapīśaḥ ।
jānāti rāma tava yuddhagatiṃ daśāsyō ।
jānāti rāma dhanadānuja ēva satyam ॥ 97
contentBlocks.translation: Hanuman knows the truth of you, O Rama. The king of monkeys (Sugriva) knows the nature of your friendship, O Rama. The ten-headed one (Ravana) knows the measure of your prowess in battle, O Rama. Truly, only the younger brother of Kubera (Vibhishana) knows you fully. (97)

contentBlocks.original:
सेव्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं श्रवणशुभकरं श्रेष्ठसुज्ञानिमन्त्रं
स्तव्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं नरकदुरितदुर्वारनिर्घातमन्त्रम् ।
भव्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं भजतु भजतु संसारनिस्तारमन्त्रं
दिव्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं दिवि भुवि विलसन्मोक्षरक्षैकमन्त्रम् ॥ 98
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sēvyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ śravaṇaśubhakaraṃ śrēṣṭhasujñānimantraṃ
stavyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ narakaduritadurvāranirghātamantram ।
bhavyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ bhajatu bhajatu saṃsāranistāramantraṃ
divyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ divi bhuvi vilasanmōkṣarakṣaikamantram ॥ 98
contentBlocks.translation: The Sri Rama mantra — worthy of service, bringing auspiciousness to the ears, the mantra of the best and wisest; praiseworthy, the mantra that uproots the terrible sins of hell; excellent, the mantra for crossing the ocean of existence — meditate on it again and again; divine, the sole mantra of liberation that shines in heaven and on earth. (98)

contentBlocks.original:
निखिलनिलयमन्त्रं नित्यतत्त्वाख्यमन्त्रं
भवकुलहरमन्त्रं भूमिजाप्राणमन्त्रम् ।
पवनजनुतमन्त्रं पार्वतीमोक्षमन्त्रं
पशुपतिनिजमन्त्रं पातु मां राममन्त्रम् ॥ 99
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nikhilanilayamantraṃ nityatattvākhyamantraṃ
bhavakulaharamantraṃ bhūmijāprāṇamantram ।
pavanajanutamantraṃ pārvatīmōkṣamantraṃ
paśupatinijamantraṃ pātu māṃ rāmamantram ॥ 99
contentBlocks.translation: May the Rama mantra protect me — the mantra that is the universal abode, the mantra of the eternal truth, the mantra that destroys the afflictions of worldly existence, the mantra that is the life of Sita (daughter of the earth), the mantra born of the son of Vayu (Hanuman), the mantra of the liberation of Parvati, the own mantra of Pashupati (Shiva). (99)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणवनिलयमन्त्रं प्राणनिर्वाणमन्त्रं
प्रकृतिपुरुषमन्त्रं ब्रह्मरुद्रेन्द्रमन्त्रम् ।
प्रकटदुरितरागद्वेषनिर्णाशमन्त्रं
रघुपतिनिजमन्त्रं रामरामेतिमन्त्रम् ॥ 100
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇavanilayamantraṃ prāṇanirvāṇamantraṃ
prakṛtipuruṣamantraṃ brahmarudrēndramantram ।
prakaṭaduritarāgadvēṣanirṇāśamantraṃ
raghupatinijamantraṃ rāmarāmētimantram ॥ 100
contentBlocks.translation: The Rama-Rama mantra — the mantra dwelling in the Pranava, the mantra of the breath's liberation, the mantra of Prakriti and Purusha, the mantra of Brahma, Rudra, and Indra, the mantra that clearly destroys passion, aversion, and manifest sin — the own mantra of Raghupati. (100)

contentBlocks.original:
दशरथसुतमन्त्रं दैत्यसंहारमन्त्रं
विबुधविनुतमन्त्रं विश्वविख्यातमन्त्रम् ।
मुनिगणनुतमन्त्रं मुक्तिमार्गैकमन्त्रं
रघुपतिनिजमन्त्रं रामरामेतिमन्त्रम् ॥ 101
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśarathasutamantraṃ daityasaṃhāramantraṃ
vibudhavinutamantraṃ viśvavikhyātamantram ।
munigaṇanutamantraṃ muktimārgaikamantraṃ
raghupatinijamantraṃ rāmarāmētimantram ॥ 101
contentBlocks.translation: The Rama-Rama mantra — the mantra of the son of Dasharatha, the mantra that destroys the demons, the mantra celebrated by all the gods, the mantra renowned throughout the universe, the mantra praised by hosts of sages, the sole mantra on the path to liberation — the own mantra of Raghupati. (101)

contentBlocks.original:
संसारसागरभयापहविश्वमन्त्रं
साक्षान्मुमुक्षुजनसेवितसिद्धमन्त्रम् ।
सारङ्गहस्तमुखहस्तनिवासमन्त्रं
कैवल्यमन्त्रमनिशं भज राममन्त्रम् ॥ 102
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsārasāgarabhayāpahaviśvamantraṃ
sākṣānmumukṣujanasēvitasiddhamantram ।
sāraṅgahastamukhahastanivāsamantraṃ
kaivalyamantramaniśaṃ bhaja rāmamantram ॥ 102
contentBlocks.translation: Always chant the Rama mantra — the universal mantra that removes the fear of the ocean of worldly existence, the accomplished mantra served by those who truly seek liberation, the mantra whose hand holds the bow and arrow, the mantra of the sole abode of liberation. (102)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु मन्त्रं जन्मसाफल्यमन्त्रं
जननमरणभेदक्लेशविच्छेदमन्त्रम् ।
सकलनिगममन्त्रं सर्वशास्त्रैकमन्त्रं
रघुपतिनिजमन्त्रं रामरामेतिमन्त्रम् ॥ 103
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu mantraṃ janmasāphalyamantraṃ
jananamaraṇabhēdaklēśavichChēdamantram ।
sakalanigamamantraṃ sarvaśāstraikamantraṃ
raghupatinijamantraṃ rāmarāmētimantram ॥ 103
contentBlocks.translation: The Rama-Rama mantra — victory, victory to the mantra that makes life fruitful, the mantra that cuts through the afflictions of birth and death; the mantra of all the Vedas, the sole mantra of all the scriptures — the own mantra of Raghupati. (103)

contentBlocks.original:
जगति विशदमन्त्रं जानकीप्राणमन्त्रं
विबुधविनुतमन्त्रं विश्वविख्यातमन्त्रम् ।
दशरथसुतमन्त्रं दैत्यसंहारमन्त्रं
रघुपतिनिजमन्त्रं रामरामेतिमन्त्रम् ॥ 104
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagati viśadamantraṃ jānakīprāṇamantraṃ
vibudhavinutamantraṃ viśvavikhyātamantram ।
daśarathasutamantraṃ daityasaṃhāramantraṃ
raghupatinijamantraṃ rāmarāmētimantram ॥ 104
contentBlocks.translation: The Rama-Rama mantra — the mantra that is pure and clear in the world, the life-mantra of Janaki (Sita), the mantra celebrated by all the gods, the mantra renowned throughout the universe, the mantra of the son of Dasharatha, the mantra that destroys the demons — the own mantra of Raghupati. (104)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मादियोगिमुनिपूजितसिद्धमन्त्रं
दारिद्र्यदुःखभवरोगविनाशमन्त्रम् ।
संसारसागरसमुत्तरणैकमन्त्रं
वन्दे महाभयहरं रघुराममन्त्रम् ॥ 105
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmādiyōgimunipūjitasiddhamantraṃ
dāridryaduḥkhabhavarōgavināśamantram ।
saṃsārasāgarasamuttaraṇaikamantraṃ
vandē mahābhayaharaṃ raghurāmamantram ॥ 105
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the great Raghunatha mantra that removes great fears — the accomplished mantra worshipped by Brahma, yogis, and great sages, the mantra that destroys the afflictions of poverty, suffering, and worldly existence, the sole mantra for crossing the ocean of worldly existence. (105)

contentBlocks.original:
शत्रुच्छेदैकमन्त्रं सरसमुपनिषद्वाक्यसम्पूज्यमन्त्रं
संसारोत्तारमन्त्रं समुचितसमये सङ्गनिर्याणमन्त्रम् ।
सर्वैश्वर्यैकमन्त्रं व्यसनभुजगसन्दष्टसन्त्राणमन्त्रं
जिह्वे श्रीराममन्त्रं जप जप सफलं जन्मसाफल्यमन्त्रम् ॥ 106
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śatruchChēdaikamantraṃ sarasamupaniṣadvākyasampūjyamantraṃ
saṃsārōttāramantraṃ samuchitasamayē saṅganiryāṇamantram ।
sarvaiśvaryaikamantraṃ vyasanabhujagasandaṣṭasantrāṇamantraṃ
jihvē śrīrāmamantraṃ japa japa saphalaṃ janmasāphalyamantram ॥ 106
contentBlocks.translation: O tongue, chant the Sri Rama mantra that yields fruit — the sole mantra for cutting down enemies, honoured by the sweet nectar-words of the Upanishads, the mantra for crossing over worldly existence, the mantra for the auspicious departure at the proper time, the sole mantra of all sovereignty, the mantra that saves one bitten by the serpent of affliction and trouble — the fruitful mantra that makes life fruitful. (106)

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं निरुपममधिकं नीतिसुज्ञानमन्त्रं
सत्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं सदमलहृदये सर्वदारोग्यमन्त्रम् ।
स्तुत्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं सुललितसुमनस्सौख्यसौभाग्यमन्त्रं
पठ्यं श्रीराममन्त्रं पवनजवरदं पातु मां राममन्त्रम् ॥ 107
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ nirupamamadhikaṃ nītisujñānamantraṃ
satyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ sadamalahṛdayē sarvadārōgyamantram ।
stutyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ sulalitasumanassaukhyasaubhāgyamantraṃ
paṭhyaṃ śrīrāmamantraṃ pavanajavaradaṃ pātu māṃ rāmamantram ॥ 107
contentBlocks.translation: May the Rama mantra protect me — ever the Sri Rama mantra, incomparably the highest, the mantra of true wisdom; truly the Sri Rama mantra, the mantra of health for ever-pure hearts; worthy of praise, the Sri Rama mantra, the mantra of lovely auspicious happiness; worthy of study, the Sri Rama mantra — the one given by the son of Vayu. (107)

contentBlocks.original:
व्यामोहप्रशमौषधं मुनिमनोवृत्तिप्रवृत्त्यौषधं
दैत्योन्मूलकरौषधं भवभयप्रध्वंसनैकौषधम् ।
भक्तानन्दकरौषधं त्रिभुवने सञ्जीवनैकौषधं
श्रेयः प्राप्तिकरौषधं पिब मनः श्रीरामनामौषधम् ॥ 108
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyāmōhapraśamauṣadhaṃ munimanōvṛttipravṛttyauṣadhaṃ
daityōnmūlakarauṣadhaṃ bhavabhayapradhvaṃsanaikauṣadham ।
bhaktānandakarauṣadhaṃ tribhuvanē sañjīvanaikauṣadhaṃ
śrēyaḥ prāptikarauṣadhaṃ piba manaḥ śrīrāmanāmauṣadham ॥ 108
contentBlocks.translation: O mind, drink the medicine of the name of Sri Rama — the medicine that calms delusion, the medicine that stirs the activity of the minds of sages, the medicine that uproots the demons, the sole medicine against the fear of worldly existence; the medicine that brings joy to devotees, the sole life-giving medicine in all three worlds, the medicine that procures the highest good. (108)

contentBlocks.original:
सकलभुवनरत्नं सर्वशास्त्रार्थरत्नं
समरविजयरत्नं सच्चिदानन्दरत्नम् ।
दशमुखहररत्नं दानवारातिरत्नं
रघुकुलनृपरत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 109
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalabhuvanaratnaṃ sarvaśāstrārtharatnaṃ
samaravijayaratnaṃ sachchidānandaratnam ।
daśamukhahararatnaṃ dānavārātiratnaṃ
raghukulanṛparatnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 109
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel of all the worlds, the jewel of the meaning of all scriptures, the jewel of victory in battle, the jewel of pure being-consciousness-bliss, the jewel that destroyed the ten-faced one (Ravana), the jewel that is the enemy of demons, the jewel-king of the Raghu dynasty. (109)

contentBlocks.original:
सकलभुवनरत्नं सच्चिदानन्दरत्नं
सकलहृदयरत्नं सूर्यबिम्बान्तरत्नम् ।
विमलसुकृतरत्नं वेदवेदान्तरत्नं
पुरहरजपरत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 110
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalabhuvanaratnaṃ sachchidānandaratnaṃ
sakalahṛdayaratnaṃ sūryabimbāntaratnam ।
vimalasukṛtaratnaṃ vēdavēdāntaratnaṃ
puraharajaparatnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 110
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel of all the worlds, the jewel of being-consciousness-bliss, the jewel of all hearts, the jewel within the solar orb, the jewel of pure merit, the jewel of the Vedas and Vedanta, the jewel of Shiva's japam. (110)

contentBlocks.original:
निगमशिखररत्नं निर्मलानन्दरत्नं
निरुपमगुणरत्नं नादनादान्तरत्नम् ।
दशरथकुलरत्नं द्वादशान्तस्स्थरत्नं
पशुपतिजपरत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 111
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nigamaśikhararatnaṃ nirmalānandaratnaṃ
nirupamaguṇaratnaṃ nādanādāntaratnam ।
daśarathakularatnaṃ dvādaśāntasstharatnaṃ
paśupatijaparatnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 111
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel at the summit of the Vedas, the jewel of unstained bliss, the jewel of incomparable virtues, the jewel at the end of the transcendent sound (Nada), the jewel of the Dasharatha dynasty, the jewel dwelling in the twelfth centre (dvadashanta), the jewel of Pashupati's japam. (111)

contentBlocks.original:
शतमखसुतरत्नं षोडशान्तस्स्थरत्नं
मुनिजनजपरत्नं मुख्यवैकुण्ठरत्नम् ।
निरुपमगुणरत्नं नीरजान्तस्स्थरत्नं
परमपदविरत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 112
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śatamakhasutaratnaṃ ṣōḍaśāntasstharatnaṃ
munijanajaparatnaṃ mukhyavaikuṇṭharatnam ।
nirupamaguṇaratnaṃ nīrajāntasstharatnaṃ
paramapadaviratnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 112
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel that is the son of Indra's son (Jayanta), the jewel dwelling in the sixteenth centre (shodashanta), the jewel worshipped by the sages, the chief jewel of Vaikuntha, the jewel of incomparable virtues, the jewel dwelling within the lotus of the heart, the jewel of the supreme abode. (112)

contentBlocks.original:
सकलसुकृतरत्नं सत्यवाक्यार्थरत्नं
शमदमगुणरत्नं शाश्वतानन्दरत्नम् ।
प्रणयनिलयरत्नं प्रस्फुटद्योतिरत्नं
परमपदविरत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 113
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalasukṛtaratnaṃ satyavākyārtharatnaṃ
śamadamaguṇaratnaṃ śāśvatānandaratnam ।
praṇayanilayaratnaṃ prasphuṭadyōtiratnaṃ
paramapadaviratnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 113
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel of all good deeds, the jewel of the meaning of truth, the jewel of the qualities of tranquillity and restraint, the jewel of eternal bliss, the jewel dwelling in the Pranava, the jewel of clear self-luminous light, the jewel of the supreme abode. (113)

contentBlocks.original:
निगमशिखररत्नं नित्यमाशास्यरत्नं
जननुतनृपरत्नं जानकीरूपरत्नम् ।
भुवनवलयरत्नं भूभुजामेकरत्नं
रघुकुलवररत्नं पातु मां रामरत्नम् ॥ 114
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nigamaśikhararatnaṃ nityamāśāsyaratnaṃ
jananutanṛparatnaṃ jānakīrūparatnam ।
bhuvanavalayaratnaṃ bhūbhujāmēkaratnaṃ
raghukulavararatnaṃ pātu māṃ rāmaratnam ॥ 114
contentBlocks.translation: May the jewel of Rama protect me — the jewel at the summit of the Vedas, the jewel ever worthy of longing, the jewel king celebrated by the people, the jewel that is the beauty of Janaki (Sita), the jewel of the circle of the worlds, the sole jewel of all kings, the finest jewel of the Raghu dynasty. (114)

contentBlocks.original:
विशालनेत्रं परिपूर्णगात्रं
सीताकलत्रं सुरवैरिजैत्रम् ।
कारुण्यपात्रं जगतः पवित्रं
श्रीरामरत्नं प्रणतोऽस्मि नित्यम् ॥ 115
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśālanētraṃ paripūrṇagātraṃ
sītākalatraṃ suravairijaitram ।
kāruṇyapātraṃ jagataḥ pavitraṃ
śrīrāmaratnaṃ praṇatō'smi nityam ॥ 115
contentBlocks.translation: Always I bow, a suppliant, to the jewel of Sri Rama — of wide and pure eyes, of wholly beautiful form, whose consort is Sita, victor over the enemies of the gods, the vessel of compassion, the purifier of the universe. (115)

contentBlocks.original:
हे गोपालक हे दयाजलनिधे हे सद्गुणाम्भोनिधे
हे दैत्यान्तक हे विभीषणदयापरीण हे भूपते ।
हे वैदेहसुतामनोजविहृते हे कोटिमाराकृते
हे नव्याम्बुजनेत्र पालय परं जानामि न त्वां विना ॥ 116
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hē gōpālaka hē dayājalanidhē hē sadguṇāmbhōnidhē
hē daityāntaka hē vibhīṣaṇadayāparīṇa hē bhūpatē ।
hē vaidēhasutāmanōjavihṛtē hē kōṭimārākṛtē
hē navyāmbujanētra pālaya paraṃ jānāmi na tvāṃ vinā ॥ 116
contentBlocks.translation: O protector of cattle (Gopala), O ocean of compassion, O ocean of true virtues, O destroyer of demons, O one full of grace toward Vibhishana, O king of the earth, O one who wanders in the garden of Vaidehi's heart, O one who has shot crores of arrows — O new-lotus-eyed one, protect me; beyond you I know nothing. (116)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य किञ्चिदपि नो हरणीयं
कर्म किञ्चिदपि नो चरणीयम् ।
रामनाम च सदा स्मरणीयं
लीलया भवजलं तरणीयम् ॥ 117
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya kiñchidapi nō haraṇīyaṃ
karma kiñchidapi nō charaṇīyam ।
rāmanāma cha sadā smaraṇīyaṃ
līlayā bhavajalaṃ taraṇīyam ॥ 117
contentBlocks.translation: For one who has nothing to be taken away, no action to be performed, the name of Rama alone is ever to be remembered — and the ocean of worldly existence is to be crossed with ease. (117)

contentBlocks.original:
दशरथसुतमीशं दण्डकारण्यवासं
शतमखमणिनीलं जानकीप्राणलोलम् ।
सकलभुवनमोहं सन्नुताम्भोददेहं
बहुलनुतसमुद्रं भावये रामभद्रम् ॥ 118
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśarathasutamīśaṃ daṇḍakāraṇyavāsaṃ
śatamakhamaṇinīlaṃ jānakīprāṇalōlam ।
sakalabhuvanamōhaṃ sannutāmbhōdadēhaṃ
bahuḻanutasamudraṃ bhāvayē rāmabhadram ॥ 118
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate Ramabhadra — the son of Dasharatha, the lord, the dweller of the Dandaka forest, dark as the blue sapphire of the sky, ever longing for the life of Janaki (Sita), the enchanter of the entire universe, of the form of the praised rain cloud, an ocean celebrated by many. (118)

contentBlocks.original:
विशालनेत्रं परिपूर्णगात्रं
सीताकलत्रं सुरवैरिजैत्रम् ।
जगत्पवित्रं परमात्मतन्त्रं
श्रीरामचन्द्रं प्रणमामि चित्ते ॥ 119
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśālanētraṃ paripūrṇagātraṃ
sītākaḻatraṃ suravairijaitram ।
jagatpavitraṃ paramātmatantraṃ
śrīrāmachandraṃ praṇamāmi chittē ॥ 119
contentBlocks.translation: I bow in my heart to Sri Ramachandra — of wide and pure eyes, of wholly beautiful form, whose consort is Sita, victor over the enemies of the gods, the purifier of the universe, the sole essence of the supreme Self. (119)

contentBlocks.original:
जय जय रघुराम श्रीमुखाम्भोजभानो
जय जय रघुवीर श्रीमदम्भोजनेत्र ।
जय जय रघुनाथ श्रीकराभ्यर्चिताङ्घ्रि
जय जय रघुवर्य श्रीश कारुण्यसिन्धो ॥ 120
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaya jaya raghurāma śrīmukhāmbhōjabhānō
jaya jaya raghuvīra śrīmadambhōjanētra ।
jaya jaya raghunātha śrīkarābhyarchitāṅghri
jaya jaya raghuvarya śrīśa kāruṇyasindhō ॥ 120
contentBlocks.translation: Victory, victory to Raghunatha, the sun of the beautiful lotus-face; victory, victory to Raghuvira of the lotus eyes filled with Sri; victory, victory to Raghunatha whose feet are worshipped by Sri's hands; victory, victory to the best of Raghus, lord Sri, ocean of compassion. (120)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दारमूले मणिपीठसंस्थं
सुधाप्लुतं दिव्यविराट्स्वरूपम् ।
सबिन्दुनादान्तकलान्ततुर्य-
मूर्तिं भजेऽहं रघुवंशरत्नम् ॥ 121
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandāramūlē maṇipīṭhasaṃsthaṃ
sudhāplutaṃ divyavirāṭsvarūpam ।
sabindunādāntakalāntaturya-
mūrtiṃ bhajē'haṃ raghuvaṃśaratnam ॥ 121
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the jewel of the Raghu clan — established on the jewelled seat beneath the coral-tree, suffused with nectar, of the divine universal form, together with the Bindu, the end of the transcendent sound (Nada), the art-essence, and the fourth state of consciousness (Turiya). (121)

contentBlocks.original:
नादं नादविनीलचित्तपवनं नादान्तत्त्वप्रियं
नामाकारविवर्जितं नवघनश्यामाङ्गनादप्रियम् ।
नादाम्भोजमरन्दमत्तविलसद्भृङ्गं मदान्तस्स्थितं
नादान्तधृवमण्डलाब्जरुचिरं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 122
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nādaṃ nādavinīlachittapavanaṃ nādāntattvapriyaṃ
nāmākāravivarjitaṃ navaghanaśyāmāṅganādapriyam ।
nādāmbhōjamarandamattavilasadbhṛṅgaṃ madāntassthitaṃ
nādāntadhṛvamaṇḍalābjaruchiraṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 122
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — who is the transcendent sound (Nada), the inner wind of the Nada-purified mind, the one who loves the truth of Nada, beyond name and form, who loves the dark-cloud-blue body of Nada, the bee intoxicated by the honey of the Nada-lotus, established within the innermost bliss, the beautiful one dwelling in the lustrous circle of the Nada's fixed point. (122)

contentBlocks.original:
नानाभूतहृदब्जपद्मनिलयं नामोज्ज्वलाभूषणम् ।
नामस्तोत्रपवित्रितत्रिभुवनं नारायणाष्टाक्षरम् ।
नादान्तेन्दुगलत्सुधाप्लुततनुं नानात्मचिन्मात्रकम् ।
नानाकोटियुगान्तभानुसदृशं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 123
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nānābhūtahṛdabjapadmanilayaṃ nāmōjjvalābhūṣaṇam ।
nāmastōtrapavitritatribhuvanaṃ nārāyaṇāṣṭākṣaram ।
nādāntēndugaḻatsudhāplutatanuṃ nānātmachinmātrakam ।
nānākōṭiyugāntabhānusadṛśaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 123
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — who dwells in the lotus-heart of all beings, who is adorned with the ornament of his name blazing with light, who purifies the three worlds through the praise of his name, who is the Narayana ashtakshara mantra, whose body is flooded with the nectar of the moon at the end of the Nada, who is the pure consciousness of all the diverse selves, who is equal to crores of suns at the end of many aeons. (123)

contentBlocks.original:
वेद्यं वेदगुरुं विरिञ्चिजनकं वेदान्तमूर्तिं स्फुर-
द्वेदं वेदकलापमूलमहिमाधारान्तकन्दाङ्कुरम् ।
वेदशृङ्गसमानशेषशयनं वेदान्तवेद्यात्मकं
वेदाराधितपादपङ्कजमहं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 124
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdyaṃ vēdaguruṃ viriñchijanakaṃ vēdāntamūrtiṃ sphura-
dvēdaṃ vēdakalāpamūlamahimādhārāntakandāṅkuram ।
vēdaśṛṅgasamānaśēṣaśayanaṃ vēdāntavēdyātmakaṃ
vēdārādhitapādapaṅkajamahaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 124
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — who is to be known, the guru of the Vedas, the father of Brahma, the very form of the Vedanta, the self-luminous Veda, the root of the grandeur of the entire collection of Vedic lore, whose bed is Shesha, equal to the peak of the Vedas, whose Self is to be known through Vedanta, whose lotus feet are worshipped by the Vedas. (124)

contentBlocks.original:
मज्जीवं मदनुग्रहं मदधिपं मद्भावनं मत्सुखं
मत्तातं मम सद्गुरुं मम वरं मोहान्धविच्छेदनम् ।
मत्पुण्यं मदनेकबान्धवजनं मज्जीवनं मन्निधिं
मत्सिद्धिं मम सर्वकर्मसुकृतं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 125
contentBlocks.transliteration:
majjīvaṃ madanugrahaṃ madadhipaṃ madbhāvanaṃ matsukhaṃ
mattātaṃ mama sadguruṃ mama varaṃ mōhāndhavichChēdanam ।
matpuṇyaṃ madanēkabāndhavajanaṃ majjīvanaṃ mannidhiṃ
matsiddhiṃ mama sarvakarmasukṛtaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 125
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — my very soul, my grace, my sovereign, my contemplation, my joy, my shelter, my true guru, my boon, the cutter of the blindness of delusion, my merit, my many kinsmen, my life, my treasure, my accomplishment, the good fruit of all my actions. (125)

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यं नीरजलोचनं निरुपमं नीवारशूकोपमं
निर्भेदानुभवं निरन्तरगुणं नीलाङ्गरागोज्ज्वलम् ।
निष्पापं निगमागमार्चितपदं नित्यात्मकं निर्मलं
निष्पुण्यं निखिलं निरञ्जनपदं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 126
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityaṃ nīrajalōchanaṃ nirupamaṃ nīvāraśūkōpamaṃ
