nirbhēdānubhavaṃ nirantaraguṇaṃ nīlāṅgarāgōjjvalam ।
niṣpāpaṃ nigamāgamārchitapadaṃ nityātmakaṃ nirmalaṃ
niṣpuṇyaṃ nikhilaṃ nirañjanapadaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 126
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — ever with eyes like water-lilies, incomparable, as fine as a grain of wild rice, of undivided experience, of unceasing qualities, resplendent with the dark-blue hue, without sin, whose feet are worshipped by Vedas and Agamas, eternal and pure, without merit, the all-pervading, of the immaculate and stainless abode. (126)

contentBlocks.original:
ध्याये त्वां हृदयाम्बुजे रघुपतिं विज्ञानदीपाङ्कुरं
हंसोहंसपरम्परादिमहिमाधारं जगन्मोहनम् ।
हस्ताम्भोजगदाब्जचक्रमतुलं पीताम्बरं कौस्तुभं
श्रीवत्सं पुरुषोत्तमं मणिनिभं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 127
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāyē tvāṃ hṛdayāmbujē raghupatiṃ vijñānadīpāṅkuraṃ
haṃsōhaṃsaparamparādimahimādhāraṃ jaganmōhanam ।
hastāmbhōjagadābjachakramatulaṃ pītāmbaraṃ kaustubhaṃ
śrīvatsaṃ puruṣōttamaṃ maṇinibhaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 127
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — whom I contemplate in the lotus of my heart as Raghupati, the sprout of the lamp of knowledge, the great support of the lineage of Ham-sa (the great syllable), the enchanter of the universe, with hands bearing the mace and lotus and discus, incomparable, clad in yellow, wearing the Kaustubha, marked with the Shrivatsa, the Purushottama of gem-like radiance. (127)

contentBlocks.original:
सत्यज्ञानमनन्तमच्युतमजं चाव्याकृतं तत्परं
कूटस्थादिसमस्तसाक्षिमनघं साक्षाद्विराट्तत्त्वदम् ।
वेद्यं विश्वमयं स्वलीनभुवनस्वाराज्यसौख्यप्रदं
पूर्णं पूर्णतरं पुराणपुरुषं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 128
contentBlocks.transliteration:
satyajñānamanantamachyutamajaṃ chāvyākṛtaṃ tatparaṃ
kūṭasthādisamastasākṣimanaghaṃ sākṣādvirāṭtattvadam ।
vēdyaṃ viśvamayaṃ svalīnabhuvanasvārājyasaukhyapradaṃ
pūrṇaṃ pūrṇataraṃ purāṇapuruṣaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 128
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — truth, consciousness, infinite, imperishable, unborn, unmanifest, supreme, Kutastha and the witness of all, sinless, the direct bestower of the Virat truth, to be known, pervading the universe, the giver of the bliss of sovereignty and happiness of all absorbed worlds, complete, supremely complete, the eternal Person. (128)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं राक्षसवंशनाशनकरं राकेन्दुबिम्बाननं
रक्षोरिं रघुवंशवर्धनकरं रक्ताधरं राघवम् ।
राधायात्मनिवासिनं रविनिभं रम्यं रमानायकं
रन्ध्रान्तर्गतशेषशायिनमहं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 129
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ rākṣasavaṃśanāśanakaraṃ rākēndubimbānanaṃ
rakṣōriṃ raghuvaṃśavardhanakaraṃ raktādharaṃ rāghavam ।
rādhāyātmanivāsinaṃ ravinibhaṃ ramyaṃ ramānāyakaṃ
randhrāntargataśēṣaśāyinamahaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 129
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — the destroyer of the demon dynasty, whose face is like the disc of the full moon, the enemy of the demons, the increaser of the Raghu dynasty, with reddish lips, Raghava, who dwells within Radha's soul, sun-like, beautiful, the lord of Rama (Lakshmi), within whom the serpent Shesha rests within the heart's aperture. (129)

contentBlocks.original:
ओतप्रोतसमस्तवस्तुनिचयं ओङ्कारबीजाक्षरं
ओङ्कारप्रकृतिं षडक्षरहितं ओङ्कारकन्दाङ्कुरम् ।
ओङ्कारस्फुटभूर्भुवस्सुपरितं ओघत्रयाराधितम्
ओङ्कारोज्ज्वलसिंहपीठनिलयं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 130
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōtaprōtasamastavastunichayaṃ ōṅkārabījākṣaraṃ
ōṅkāraprakṛtiṃ ṣaḍakṣarahitaṃ ōṅkārakandāṅkuram ।
ōṅkārasphuṭabhūrbhuvassuparitaṃ ōghatrayārādhitam
ōṅkārōjjvalasiṃhapīṭhanilayaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 130
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — the aggregate of all things woven together, the seed-syllable of the Omkara, the essence of the Pranava, bearing the six syllables, the sprout of the Omkara seed, clearly pervading the Bhuh, Bhuvah, Svah — the three worlds of the Pranava, worshipped by the three floods, whose abode is the lustrous lion-throne of the Pranava. (130)

contentBlocks.original:
साकेते नगरे समस्तसुखदे हर्म्येऽब्जकोटिद्युते
नक्षत्रग्रहपङ्क्तिलग्नशिखरे चान्तर्यपङ्केरुहे ।
वाल्मीकात्रिपराशरादिमुनिभिस्संसेव्यमानं स्थितं
सीतालङ्कृतवामभागमनिशं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 131
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākētē nagarē samastasukhadē harmyē'bjakōṭidyutē
nakṣatragrahapaṅktilagnaśikharē chāntaryapaṅkēruhē ।
vālmīkātriparāśarādimunibhissaṃsēvyamānaṃ sthitaṃ
sītālaṅkṛtavāmabhāgamaniśaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 131
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — unceasingly — established in Ayodhya, the city that grants all happiness, in a palace blazing with the light of crores of lotuses, whose summit touches the rows of stars and planets, at the inner lotus of the universe, who is waited upon by Valmiki, Atri, Parashara, and other sages, with Sita adorning his left side. (131)

contentBlocks.original:
वैकुण्ठे नगरे सुरद्रुमतले चानन्दवप्रान्तरे
नानारत्नविनिर्मितस्फुटपटुप्राकारसंवेष्टिते ।
सौधेन्दूपलशेषतल्पललिते नीलोत्पलच्छादिते
पर्यङ्के शयनं रमादिसहितं रामं भजे तारकम् ॥ 132
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaikuṇṭhē nagarē suradrumatalē chānandavaprāntarē
nānāratnavinirmitasphuṭapaṭuprākārasaṃvēṣṭitē ।
saudhēndūpalaśēṣatalpalalitē nīlōtpalachChāditē
paryaṅkē śayanaṃ ramādisahitaṃ rāmaṃ bhajē tārakam ॥ 132
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the saviour (Tara) — in Vaikuntha, the divine city, beneath the wish-fulfilling tree, in the pleasure-grove of bliss, surrounded by a clear and brilliant wall of many jewels, on the beautiful moon-stone bed of Shesha, covered with dark blue lotus-petals, reclining together with Lakshmi and the others. (132)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे राममनादिपूरुषमजं वन्दे रमानायकं
वन्दे हारिकिरीटकुण्डलधरं वन्दे सुनीलद्युतिम् ।
वन्दे चापकलम्बकोज्ज्वलकरं वन्दे जगन्मङ्गलं
वन्दे पङ्क्तिरथात्मजं मम गुरुं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 133
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē rāmamanādipūruṣamajaṃ vandē ramānāyakaṃ
vandē hārikirīṭakuṇḍaladharaṃ vandē sunīladyutim ।
vandē chāpakalambakōjjvalakaraṃ vandē jaganmaṅgaḻaṃ
vandē paṅktirathātmajaṃ mama guruṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 133
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Rama, always, the Raghava — the beginningless Person, unborn; I bow to the lord of Lakshmi; I bow to the wearer of the beautiful crown and earrings; I bow to the one of dark blue radiance; I bow to the one whose hand blazes with the bow and quiver; I bow to the auspicious one of the universe; I bow to the son of he of the row of chariots (Dasharatha), my guru. (133)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे शौनकगौतमाद्यभिनुतं वन्दे घनश्यामलं
वन्दे तारकपीठमध्यनिलयं वन्दे जगन्नायकम् ।
वन्दे भक्तजनौघदेविवटपं वन्दे धनुर्वल्लभं
वन्दे तत्त्वमसीतिवाक्यजनकं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 134
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē śaunakagautamādyabhinutaṃ vandē ghanaśyāmalaṃ
vandē tārakapīṭhamadhyanilayaṃ vandē jagannāyakam ।
vandē bhaktajanaughadēvivaṭapaṃ vandē dhanurvallabhaṃ
vandē tattvamasītivākyajanakaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 134
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — praised by Shaunaka, Gautama, and others; I bow to the dark rain cloud; I bow to the one dwelling in the centre of the Tara-seat; I bow to the lord of the universe; I bow to the wish-fulfilling tree for the hosts of devotees; I bow to the lover of the bow; I bow to the father of the Vedic pronouncement "tat tvam asi." (134)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे सूर्यशशाङ्कलोचनयुगं वन्दे जगत्पावनं
वन्दे पत्रसहस्रपद्मनिलयं वन्दे पुरारिप्रियम् ।
वन्दे राक्षसवंशनाशनकरं वन्दे सुधाशीतलं
वन्दे देवकपीन्द्रकोटिविनुतं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 135
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē sūryaśaśāṅkalōchanayugaṃ vandē jagatpāvanaṃ
vandē patrasahasrapadmanilayaṃ vandē purāripriyam ।
vandē rākṣasavaṃśanāśanakaraṃ vandē sudhāśītalaṃ
vandē dēvakapīndrakōṭivinutaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 135
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — with sun and moon as his two eyes; I bow to the purifier of the universe; I bow to the one dwelling in the thousand-petalled lotus; I bow to the dear one of the enemy of the triple-city (Shiva); I bow to the destroyer of the demon dynasty; I bow to the one cool as nectar; I bow to the one praised by crores of gods and monkey lords. (135)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे सागरगर्वभङ्गविशिखं वन्दे जगज्जीवनं
वन्दे कौशिकयागरक्षणकरं वन्दे गुरुणां गुरुम् ।
वन्दे बाणशरासनोज्ज्वलकरं वन्दे जटावल्कलं
वन्दे लक्ष्मणभूमिजान्वितमहं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 136
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē sāgaragarvabhaṅgaviśikhaṃ vandē jagajjīvanaṃ
vandē kauśikayāgarakṣaṇakaraṃ vandē guruṇāṃ gurum ।
vandē bāṇaśarāsanōjjvalakaraṃ vandē jaṭāvalkalaṃ
vandē lakṣmaṇabhūmijānvitamahaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 136
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — who bears the arrow that broke the pride of the ocean; I bow to the life of the universe; I bow to the protector of Vishvamitra's sacrifice; I bow to the guru of gurus; I bow to the one whose hand blazes with bow and arrows; I bow to the one clad in bark and matted hair; I bow to the one accompanied by Lakshmana and Sita. (136)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे पाण्डरपुण्डरीकनयनं वन्देऽब्जबिम्बाननं
वन्दे कम्बुगलं कराब्जयुगलं वन्दे ललाटोज्ज्वलम् ।
वन्दे पीतदुकूलमम्बुदनिभं वन्दे जगन्मोहनं
वन्दे कारणमानुषोज्ज्वलतनुं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 137
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē pāṇḍarapuṇḍarīkanayanaṃ vandē'bjabimbānanaṃ
vandē kambugaḻaṃ karābjayugaḻaṃ vandē lalāṭōjjvalam ।
vandē pītadukūlamambudanibhaṃ vandē jaganmōhanaṃ
vandē kāraṇamānuṣōjjvalatanuṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 137
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — of white lotus eyes; I bow to the one whose face is like the lotus orb; I bow to the one with the conch-shell neck and lotus-pair of hands; I bow to the one with the brilliant forehead; I bow to the one in yellow silk, dark as a rain cloud; I bow to the enchanter of the universe; I bow to the one whose body shines as the causal human form. (137)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे नीलसरोजकोमलरुचिं वन्दे जगद्वन्दितं
वन्दे सूर्यकुलाब्धिकौस्तुभमणिं वन्दे सुराराधितम् ।
वन्दे पातकपञ्चकप्रहरणं वन्दे जगत्कारणं
वन्दे विंशतिपञ्चतत्त्वरहितं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 138
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē nīlasarōjakōmalaruchiṃ vandē jagadvanditaṃ
vandē sūryakulābdhikaustubhamaṇiṃ vandē surārādhitam ।
vandē pātakapañchakapraharaṇaṃ vandē jagatkāraṇaṃ
vandē viṃśatipañchatattvarahitaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 138
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — of the gentle beauty of the dark blue lotus; I bow to the one worshipped by the universe; I bow to the Kaustubha gem of the ocean of the solar dynasty; I bow to the one worshipped by the gods; I bow to the destroyer of the five groups of sins; I bow to the cause of the universe; I bow to the one free from the twenty-five tattvas. (138)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे साधकवर्गकल्पकतरुं वन्दे त्रिमूर्त्यात्मकं
वन्दे नादलयान्तरस्थलगतं वन्दे त्रिवर्गात्मकम् ।
वन्दे रागविहीनचित्तसुलभं वन्दे सभानायकं
वन्दे पूर्णदयामृतार्णवमहं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 139
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē sādhakavargakalpakataruṃ vandē trimūrtyātmakaṃ
vandē nādalayāntarasthalagataṃ vandē trivargātmakam ।
vandē rāgavihīnachittasulabhaṃ vandē sabhānāyakaṃ
vandē pūrṇadayāmṛtārṇavamahaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 139
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — the wish-fulfilling tree for the community of seekers; I bow to the one who embodies the three Murti-forms (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva); I bow to the one who dwells in the end of the Nada and Laya; I bow to the one who is the three goals of life; I bow to the one easily found by a mind free from passion; I bow to the lord of the assembly; I bow to the infinite ocean of compassionate nectar. (139)

contentBlocks.original:
वन्दे सात्त्विकतत्त्वमुद्रिततनुं वन्दे सुधादायकं
वन्दे चारुचतुर्भुजं मणिनिभं वन्दे षडब्जस्थितम् ।
वन्दे ब्रह्मपिपीलिकादिनिलयं वन्दे विराट्विग्रहं
वन्दे पन्नगतल्पशायिनमहं वन्दे सदा राघवम् ॥ 140
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vandē sāttvikatattvamudritatanuṃ vandē sudhādāyakaṃ
vandē chāruchaturbhujaṃ maṇinibhaṃ vandē ṣaḍabjasthitam ।
vandē brahmapipīlikādinilayaṃ vandē virāṭvigrahaṃ
vandē pannagatalpaśāyinamahaṃ vandē sadā rāghavam ॥ 140
contentBlocks.translation: I bow always to the Raghava — whose body is impressed with the sattvic truth; I bow to the giver of nectar; I bow to the beautiful four-armed one of gem-like radiance; I bow to the one dwelling in the six lotuses; I bow to the one whose abode extends from Brahma to the tiniest ant; I bow to the cosmic Virat form; I bow to the one who rests upon the serpent-couch. (140)

contentBlocks.original:
सिंहासनस्थं मुनिसिद्धसेव्यं
रक्तोत्पलालङ्कृतपादपद्मम् ।
सीतासमेतं शशिसूर्यनेत्रं
रामं भजे राघवरामचन्द्रम् ॥ 141
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siṃhāsanasthaṃ munisiddhasēvyaṃ
raktōtpalālaṅkṛtapādapadmam ।
sītāsamētaṃ śaśisūryanētraṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghavarāmachandram ॥ 141
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — enthroned on the lion-seat, worthy of adoration by sages and perfected beings, whose lotus feet are adorned with red lotus flowers, attended by Sita, with the moon and sun as his two eyes. (141)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामभद्राश्रितसद्गुरूणां
पादारविन्दं भजतां नराणाम् ।
आरोग्यमैश्वर्यमनन्तकीर्ति-
रन्ते च विष्णोः पदमस्ति सत्यम् ॥ 142
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmabhadrāśritasadgurūṇāṃ
pādāravindaṃ bhajatāṃ narāṇām ।
ārōgyamaiśvaryamanantakīrti-
rantē cha viṣṇōḥ padamasti satyam ॥ 142
contentBlocks.translation: Truly, for those men who worship the lotus feet of the good gurus devoted to Sri Ramabhadra — health, sovereignty, infinite fame, and at last the abode of Vishnu, are certain. (142)

contentBlocks.original:
दशरथवरपुत्रं जानकीसत्कलत्रं
दशमुखहरदक्षं पद्मपत्रायताक्षम् ।
करधृतशरचापं चारुमुक्ताकलापं
रघुकुलनृवरेण्यं राममीडे शरण्यम् ॥ 143
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśarathavaraputraṃ jānakīsatkaḻatraṃ
daśamukhaharadakṣaṃ padmapatrāyatākṣam ।
karadhṛtaśarachāpaṃ chārumuktākalāpaṃ
raghukulanṛvarēṇyaṃ rāmamīḍē śaraṇyam ॥ 143
contentBlocks.translation: I praise and take refuge in Rama — the noble son of Dasharatha, the dear consort of Janaki (Sita), expert at slaying the ten-faced one, with eyes wide as lotus petals, bearing a bow and arrows in his lotus hands, wearing a lovely pearl garland, the best king of the Raghu dynasty, the granter of refuge. (143)

contentBlocks.original:
दशमुखगजसिंहं दैत्यगर्वातिरंहं
कदनभयदहस्तं तारकब्रह्म शस्तम् ।
मणिखचितकिरीटं मञ्जुलालापवाटं
दशरथकुलचन्द्रं रामचन्द्रं भजेऽहम् ॥ 144
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśamukhagajasiṃhaṃ daityagarvātiraṃhaṃ
kadanabhayadahastaṃ tārakabrahma śastam ।
maṇikhachitakirīṭaṃ mañjulālāpavāṭaṃ
daśarathakulachandraṃ rāmachandraṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 144
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Ramachandra, the moon of the Dasharatha dynasty — the lion to the elephant of the ten-faced demon, swift to punish the pride of the demons, bearing weapons for the destruction of fear, praised as the Tara-Brahman, wearing a gem-inlaid crown, with a lovely and melodious speech. (144)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं रक्तसरोरुहाक्षममलं लङ्काधिनाथान्तकं
कौसल्यानयनोत्सुकं रघुवरं नागेन्द्रतल्पस्थितम् ।
वैदेहीकुचकुम्भकुङ्कुमरजोलङ्कारहारं हरिं
मायामानुषविग्रहं रघुपतिं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 145
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ raktasarōruhākṣamamalaṃ laṅkādhināthāntakaṃ
kausalyānayanōtsukaṃ raghuvaraṃ nāgēndratalpasthitam ।
vaidēhīkuchakumbhakuṅkumarajōlaṅkārahāraṃ hariṃ
māyāmānuṣavigrahaṃ raghupatiṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 145
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghupati together with Sita — of pure red lotus eyes, the destroyer of the lord of Lanka, the eager joy of Kausalya's eyes, reclining on the great serpent's couch, whose chest is marked by the saffron of Vaidehi's bosom, Hari, who has assumed the maya form of a human. (145)

contentBlocks.original:
रामं राक्षसमर्दनं रघुवरं दैतेयभिध्वंसिनं
सुग्रीवेप्सितराज्यदं सुरपतेर्भीत्यन्तकं शार्‍ङ्गिणम् ।
भक्तानामभयप्रदं भयहरं पापौघविध्वंसिनं
सामीरिस्तुतपादपद्मयुगलं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 146
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmaṃ rākṣasamardanaṃ raghuvaraṃ daitēyabhidhvaṃsinaṃ
sugrīvēpsitarājyadaṃ surapatērbhītyantakaṃ śār‍ṅgiṇam ।
bhaktānāmabhayapradaṃ bhayaharaṃ pāpaughavidhvaṃsinaṃ
sāmīristutapādapadmayugaḻaṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 146
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita — the destroyer of demons, the lord of the Raghus, the slayer of the race of demons, who granted Sugriva his longed-for kingdom, who ended the fear of the lord of the gods (Indra), bearing the Sharnga bow, who grants fearlessness to his devotees and destroys fear, who wipes away multitudes of sin, whose lotus feet are praised by the ocean (Sagara). (146)

contentBlocks.original:
यत्पादाम्बुजरेणुना मुनिसती मुक्तिङ्गता यन्महः
पुण्यं पातकनाशनं त्रिजगतां भाति स्मृतं पावनम् ।
स्मृत्वा राघवमप्रमेयममलं पूर्णेन्दुमन्दस्मितं
तं रामं सरसीरुहाक्षममलं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 147
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yatpādāmbujarēṇunā munisatī muktiṅgatā yanmahaḥ
puṇyaṃ pātakanāśanaṃ trijagatāṃ bhāti smṛtaṃ pāvanam ।
smṛtvā rāghavamapramēyamamalaṃ pūrṇēndumandasmitaṃ
taṃ rāmaṃ sarasīruhākṣamamalaṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 147
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita — through the dust of whose lotus feet the sage's wife attained liberation, whose greatness is holy and destroys the sins of the three worlds when remembered and is ever purifying; Raghava, immeasurable and pure, with the gentle smile of the full moon, of pure lotus eyes. (147)

contentBlocks.original:
वैदेहीकुचमण्डलाग्र-विलसन्माणिक्यहस्ताम्बुजं
चञ्चत्कङ्कणहारनूपुर-लसत्केयूरहारान्वितम् ।
दिव्यश्रीमणिकुण्डलोज्ज्वल-महाभूषासहस्रान्वितं
वीरश्रीरघुपुङ्गवं गुणनिधिं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 148
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaidēhīkuchamaṇḍalāgra-vilasanmāṇikyahastāmbujaṃ
chañchatkaṅkaṇahāranūpura-lasatkēyūrahārānvitam ।
divyaśrīmaṇikuṇḍalōjjvala-mahābhūṣāsahasrānvitaṃ
vīraśrīraghupuṅgavaṃ guṇanidhiṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 148
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Raghupati, the treasury of virtues, together with Sita — whose lotus hands rest on the rounded bosom of Vaidehi adorned with rubies, adorned with bracelets, necklaces, anklets, armlets, and garlands all gleaming, adorned with thousands of divine and magnificent ornaments — the heroic and glorious one. (148)

contentBlocks.original:
वैदेहीकुचमण्डलोपरि-लसन्माणिक्यहारावली-
मध्यस्थं नवनीतकोमलरुचिं नीलोत्पलश्यामलम् ।
कन्दर्पायुतकोटिसुन्दरतनुं पूर्णेन्दुबिम्बाननं
कौसल्याकुलभूषणं रघुपतिं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 149
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaidēhīkuchamaṇḍalōpari-lasanmāṇikyahārāvaḻī-
madhyasthaṃ navanītakōmalaruchiṃ nīlōtpalaśyāmalam ।
kandarpāyutakōṭisundaratanuṃ pūrṇēndubimbānanaṃ
kausalyākulabhūṣaṇaṃ raghupatiṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 149
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghupati together with Sita — who is established in the midst of the rows of ruby garlands on Vaidehi's bosom, tender and gentle as fresh butter, dark as the blue lotus, whose body is equal in beauty to ten million Kamadevs, whose face is like the orb of the full moon, the ornament of the family of Kausalya. (149)

contentBlocks.original:
दिव्यारण्ययतीन्द्रनामनगरे मध्ये महामण्टपे
स्वर्णस्तम्भसहस्रषोडशयुते मन्दारमूलाश्रिते ।
नानारत्नविचित्रनिर्मलमहासिंहासने संस्थितं
सीतालक्ष्मणसेवितं रघुपतिं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 150
contentBlocks.transliteration:
divyāraṇyayatīndranāmanagarē madhyē mahāmaṇṭapē
svarṇastambhasahasraṣōḍaśayutē mandāramūlāśritē ।
nānāratnavichitranirmalamahāsiṃhāsanē saṃsthitaṃ
sītālakṣmaṇasēvitaṃ raghupatiṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 150
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghupati together with Sita — in the great hall of the divine forest-dweller's city, seated on a magnificent lion-throne of pure and varied precious stones, in a palace with sixteen thousand golden pillars, at the base of the coral-tree, served by Lakshmana. (150)

contentBlocks.original:
कस्तूरीतिलकं कपीन्द्रहरणं कारुण्यवारान्निधिं
क्षीराम्भोधिसुतामुखाब्जमधुपं कल्याणसम्पन्निधिम् ।
कौसल्यानयनोत्सुकं कपिवरत्राणं महापौरुषं
कौमारप्रियमर्ककोटिसदृशं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 151
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kastūrītilakaṃ kapīndraharaṇaṃ kāruṇyavārānnidhiṃ
kṣīrāmbhōdhisutāmukhābjamadhupaṃ kalyāṇasampannidhim ।
kausalyānayanōtsukaṃ kapivaratrāṇaṃ mahāpauruṣaṃ
kaumārapriyamarkakōṭisadṛśaṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 151
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita — marked with the musk tilaka, who ravished the king of monkeys (Sugriva's heart), an ocean full of compassion, the bee at the lotus-face of the daughter of the ocean of milk (Lakshmi), the treasury of auspiciousness, the eager joy of Kausalya's eyes, the protector of the best monkeys, of great heroism, equal in brilliance to crores of suns of the dawn. (151)

contentBlocks.original:
विद्युत्कोटिदिवाकरद्युतिनिभं श्रीकौस्तुभालङ्कृतं
योगीन्द्रैस्सनकादिभिः परिवृतं कैलासनाथप्रियम् ।
मुक्तारत्नकिरीटकुण्डलधरं ग्रैवेयहारान्वितं
वैदेहीकुचसन्निवासमनिशं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 152
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidyutkōṭidivākaradyutinibhaṃ śrīkaustubhālaṅkṛtaṃ
yōgīndraissanakādibhiḥ parivṛtaṃ kailāsanāthapriyam ।
muktāratnakirīṭakuṇḍaladharaṃ graivēyahārānvitaṃ
vaidēhīkuchasannivāsamaniśaṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 152
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita unceasingly — of the radiance of crores of lightnings and suns, adorned with the Sri Kaustubha gem, surrounded by Yogindras led by Sanaka, dear to the lord of Kailasa (Shiva), wearing a pearl-gem crown and earrings, adorned with a neck-ornament and garland, whose abode is the bosom of Vaidehi. (152)

contentBlocks.original:
मेघश्यामलमम्बुजातनयनं विस्तीर्णवक्षस्स्थलं
बाहुद्वन्द्वविराजितं सुवदनं शोणाङ्घ्रिपङ्केरुहम् ।
नानारत्नविचित्रभूषणयुतं कोदण्डबाणाङ्कितं
त्रैलोक्याऽप्रतिमानसुन्दरतनुं सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 153
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mēghaśyāmalamambujātanayanaṃ vistīrṇavakṣassthalaṃ
bāhudvandvavirājitaṃ suvadanaṃ śōṇāṅghripaṅkēruham ।
nānāratnavichitrabhūṣaṇayutaṃ kōdaṇḍabāṇāṅkitaṃ
trailōkyā'pratimānasundaratanuṃ sītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 153
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita — dark blue as a rain cloud, with wide lotus eyes, with a broad and expansive chest, with two radiant arms, of beautiful face, with lotus-like red feet, adorned with various and wondrous jewels, bearing the bow and arrows, of unparalleled beauty of form in all three worlds. (153)

contentBlocks.original:
वैदेहीयुतवामभागमतुलं वन्दारुमन्दारकं
वन्दे प्रस्तुतकीर्तिवासिततरुच्छायानुकारिप्रभम् ।
वैदेहीकुचकुङ्कुमाङ्कितमहोरस्कं महाभूषणं
वेदान्तैरुपगीयमानमसकृत्सीतासमेतं भजे ॥ 154
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaidēhīyutavāmabhāgamatulaṃ vandārumandārakaṃ
vandē prastutakīrtivāsitataruchChāyānukāriprabham ।
vaidēhīkuchakuṅkumāṅkitamahōraskaṃ mahābhūṣaṇaṃ
vēdāntairupagīyamānamasakṛtsītāsamētaṃ bhajē ॥ 154
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Rama together with Sita unceasingly — with Vaidehi adorning his left side, incomparable, the wish-fulfilling tree for those who bow, whose radiance imitates the shadow of a tree with renowned fame spread wide, whose broad chest is marked with the saffron of Vaidehi's bosom, adorned with magnificent ornaments, sung of again and again by the Vedanta. (154)

contentBlocks.original:
देवानां हितकारणेन भुवने धृत्वाऽवतारं ध्रुवं
रामं कौशिकयज्ञविघ्नदलनं तत्ताटकासंहरम् ।
नित्यं गौतमपत्निशापदलनश्रीपादरेणुं शुभं
शम्भोरुत्कटचापखण्डनमहासत्वं भजे राघवम् ॥ 155
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvānāṃ hitakāraṇēna bhuvanē dhṛtvā'vatāraṃ dhruvaṃ
rāmaṃ kauśikayajñavighnadalanaṃ tattāṭakāsaṃharam ।
nityaṃ gautamapatniśāpadalanaśrīpādarēṇuṃ śubhaṃ
śambhōrutkaṭachāpakhaṇḍanamahāsatvaṃ bhajē rāghavam ॥ 155
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Raghava — who certainly took his incarnation on earth for the benefit of the gods, who protected the sacrifice of Vishvamitra, who destroyed Tataka, whose feet-dust instantly released the wife of Gautama from her curse, who broke the immense and terrible bow of Shambhu (Shiva) — of pure and auspicious form. (155)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामं नवरत्नकुण्डलधरं श्रीरामरक्षामणिं
श्रीरामं च सहस्रभानुसदृशं श्रीरामचन्द्रोदयम् ।
श्रीरामं श्रुतकीर्तिमाकरमहं श्रीराममुक्तिप्रदं
श्रीरामं रघुनन्दनं भयहरं श्रीरामचन्द्रं भजे ॥ 156
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmaṃ navaratnakuṇḍaladharaṃ śrīrāmarakṣāmaṇiṃ
śrīrāmaṃ cha sahasrabhānusadṛśaṃ śrīrāmachandrōdayam ।
śrīrāmaṃ śrutakīrtimākaramahaṃ śrīrāmamuktipradaṃ
śrīrāmaṃ raghunandanaṃ bhayaharaṃ śrīrāmachandraṃ bhajē ॥ 156
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sri Ramachandra — Sri Rama wearing nine-gem earrings, Sri Rama the protective jewel, Sri Rama equal in brightness to a thousand suns, the rising of the moon Ramachandra, Sri Rama the great ocean of renowned virtue, Sri Rama the giver of liberation, Sri Rama the delight of Raghu's lineage, who removes fear. (156)

contentBlocks.original:
राममिन्दीवरश्यामं राजीवायतलोचनम् ।
ज्याघोषनिर्जितारातिं जानकीरमणं भजे ॥ 157
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmamindīvaraśyāmaṃ rājīvāyatalōchanam ।
jyāghōṣanirjitārātiṃ jānakīramaṇaṃ bhajē ॥ 157
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Janaki's beloved Rama — dark as the blue lotus, with wide lotus eyes, whose enemies are defeated by the twang of his bowstring. (157)

contentBlocks.original:
दीर्घबाहुमरविन्दलोचनं
दीनवत्सलमनाथरक्षकम् ।
दीक्षितं सकललोकरक्षणे
दैवतं दशरथात्मजं भजे ॥ 158
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīrghabāhumaravindalōchanaṃ
dīnavatsalamanātharakṣakam ।
dīkṣitaṃ sakalalōkarakṣaṇē
daivataṃ daśarathātmajaṃ bhajē ॥ 158
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the son of Dasharatha — long-armed, with lotus eyes, compassionate to the lowly, protector of the helpless, consecrated to the protection of all the worlds, the deity of all. (158)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातस्स्मरामि रघुनाथमुखारविन्दं
मन्दस्मितं मधुरभाषि विशालफालम् ।
कर्णावलम्बिचलकुण्डलगण्डभागं
कर्णान्तदीर्घनयनं नयनाभिरामम् ॥ 159
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātassmarāmi raghunāthamukhāravindaṃ
mandasmitaṃ madhurabhāṣi viśālaphālam ।
karṇāvalambichalakuṇḍalagaṇḍabhāgaṃ
karṇāntadīrghanayanaṃ nayanābhirāmam ॥ 159
contentBlocks.translation: In the morning I remember the lotus-face of Raghunatha — of gentle smile, speaking sweetly, with a wide and beautiful forehead, whose cheeks are adorned with swinging earrings at the ears, with eyes long as to the very ears — a feast for the eyes. (159)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातर्भजामि रघुनाथकरारविन्दं
रक्षोगणाय भयदं वरदं निजेभ्यः ।
यद्राजसंसदि विभिद्य महेशचापं
सीताकरग्रहणमङ्गलमाप सद्यः ॥ 160
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātarbhajāmi raghunāthakarāravindaṃ
rakṣōgaṇāya bhayadaṃ varadaṃ nijēbhyaḥ ।
yadrājasaṃsadi vibhidya mahēśachāpaṃ
sītākaragrahaṇamaṅgaḻamāpa sadyaḥ ॥ 160
contentBlocks.translation: In the morning I worship the lotus hands of Raghunatha — which strike fear into the demon armies and grant boons to his own; which at once broke the great bow of Maheshvara in the royal assembly and auspiciously won Sita's hand in marriage. (160)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातर्नमामि रघुनाथपदारविन्दं
पद्माङ्कुशादिशुभरेखशुभावहं च ।
योगीन्द्रमानसमधुव्रतसेव्यमानं
शापापहं सपदि गौतमधर्मपत्न्याः ॥ 161
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātarnamāmi raghunāthapadāravindaṃ
padmāṅkuśādiśubharēkhaśubhāvahaṃ cha ।
yōgīndramānasamadhuvratasēvyamānaṃ
śāpāpahaṃ sapadi gautamadharmapatnyāḥ ॥ 161
contentBlocks.translation: In the morning I bow to the lotus feet of Raghunatha — adorned with the auspicious marks of the lotus, goad, and other signs, contemplated by the best of yogis like bees at a lotus, which instantly released the wife of Gautama from her curse. (161)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातर्वदामि वचसा रघुनाथनाम
वाग्दोषहारि सकलं कलुषं निहन्तृ ।
यत्पार्वती स्वपतिना सह भोक्तुकामा
प्रीत्या सहस्रहरिनामसमं जजाप ॥ 162
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātarvadāmi vachasā raghunāthanāma
vāgdōṣahāri sakalaṃ kaluṣaṃ nihantṛ ।
yatpārvatī svapatinā saha bhōktukāmā
prītyā sahasraharināmasamaṃ jajāpa ॥ 162
contentBlocks.translation: In the morning I speak with my tongue the name of Raghunatha — which removes all the flaws of speech and destroys every impurity; which Parvati, desiring to enjoy it with her lord, lovingly chanted as equal to a thousand names of Hari. (162)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातः श्रये श्रुतिनुतं रघुनाथमूर्तिं
नीलाम्बुदोत्पलसितेतररत्ननीलाम् ।
आमुक्तमौक्तिकविशेषविभूषणाढ्यां
ध्येयां समस्तमुनिभिर्निजभृत्यमुख्यैः ॥ 163
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātaḥ śrayē śrutinutaṃ raghunāthamūrtiṃ
nīlāmbudōtpalasitētararatnanīlām ।
āmuktamauktikaviśēṣavibhūṣaṇāḍhyāṃ
dhyēyāṃ samastamunibhirnijabhṛtyamukhyaiḥ ॥ 163
contentBlocks.translation: In the morning I take refuge in the form of Raghunatha, celebrated by the Vedas — dark blue as a rain cloud, lotus, and sapphire; adorned with special pearl ornaments, worthy of contemplation by all sages and by the foremost of his servants. (163)

contentBlocks.original:
रघुकुलवरनाथो जानकीप्राणनाथः
पितृवचनविधाता कीशराज्यप्रदाता ।
प्रतिनिशिचरनाशः प्राप्तराज्यप्रवेशो
विहितभुवनरक्षः पातु पद्मायताक्षः ॥ 164
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raghukulavaranāthō jānakīprāṇanāthaḥ
pitṛvachanavidhātā kīśarājyapradātā ।
pratiniśicharanāśaḥ prāptarājyapravēśō
vihitabhuvanarakṣaḥ pātu padmāyatākṣaḥ ॥ 164
contentBlocks.translation: May the lotus-eyed one protect me — the best lord of the Raghu dynasty, the lord of Janaki's life, the fulfiller of his father's word, the giver of the kingdom to the king of monkeys (Sugriva), the destroyer of demon after demon, who reclaimed the kingdom and entered it, who is established as the protector of the universe. (164)

contentBlocks.original:
कुवलयदलनीलः पीतवासाः स्मितास्यो
विविधरुचिरभूषाभूषितो दिव्यमूर्तिः ।
दशरथकुलनाथो जानकीप्राणनाथो
निवसतु मम चित्ते सर्वदा रामचन्द्रः ॥ 165
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuvalayadaḻanīlaḥ pītavāsāḥ smitāsyō
vividharuchirabhūṣābhūṣitō divyamūrtiḥ ।
daśarathakulanāthō jānakīprāṇanāthō
nivasatu mama chittē sarvadā rāmachandraḥ ॥ 165
contentBlocks.translation: May Ramachandra ever dwell in my heart — dark blue as the petal of the blue lotus, clad in yellow silk, of smiling countenance, of wondrous divine form adorned with varied beautiful ornaments, the chief lord of the Dasharatha dynasty, the lord of Janaki's life. (165)

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु रामो जानकीवल्लभोऽयं
जयतु जयतु रामश्चन्द्रचूडार्चिताङ्घ्रिः ।
जयतु जयतु वाणीनाथनाथः परात्मा
जयतु जयतु रामोऽनाथनाथः कृपालुः ॥ 166
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu rāmō jānakīvallabhō'yaṃ
jayatu jayatu rāmaśchandrachūḍārchitāṅghriḥ ।
jayatu jayatu vāṇīnāthanāthaḥ parātmā
jayatu jayatu rāmō'nāthanāthaḥ kṛpāḻuḥ ॥ 166
contentBlocks.translation: Victory, victory to Rama, the beloved of Janaki; victory, victory to Rama whose feet are worshipped by the one bearing the crescent moon (Shiva); victory, victory to Rama, the lord of Vani's lord, the supreme Self; victory, victory to Rama, the lord of the helpless, full of compassion. (166)

contentBlocks.original:
वदतु वदतु वाणी रामरामेति नित्यं
जयतु जयतु चित्तं रामपादारविन्दम् ।
नमतु नमतु देहं सन्ततं रामचन्द्रं
न भवतु मम पापं जन्मजन्मान्तरेषु ॥ 167
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vadatu vadatu vāṇī rāmarāmēti nityaṃ
jayatu jayatu chittaṃ rāmapādāravindam ।
namatu namatu dēhaṃ santataṃ rāmachandraṃ
na bhavatu mama pāpaṃ janmajanmāntarēṣu ॥ 167
contentBlocks.translation: May speech ever speak "Rama, Rama"; may the heart rejoice in the lotus feet of Rama; may the body bow again and again to Ramachandra — may there be no sin in me birth after birth. (167)

contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दरूपं वरदं प्रसन्नं
सिंहेक्षणं सेवकपारिजातम् ।
नीलोत्पलाङ्गं भुवनैकमित्रं
रामं भजे राघवरामचन्द्रम् ॥ 168
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandarūpaṃ varadaṃ prasannaṃ
siṃhēkṣaṇaṃ sēvakapārijātam ।
nīlōtpalāṅgaṃ bhuvanaikamitraṃ
rāmaṃ bhajē rāghavarāmachandram ॥ 168
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Rama, the Raghava, the Ramachandra — of the form of bliss, the gracious boon-giver, with the gaze of a lion, the wish-fulfilling tree for his servants, dark as the blue lotus, the sole friend of all the worlds. (168)

contentBlocks.original:
लङ्काविरामं रणरङ्गभीमं
राजीवनेत्रं रघुवंशमित्रम् ।
कारुण्यमूर्तिं करुणाप्रपूर्तिं
श्रीरामचन्द्रं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 169
contentBlocks.transliteration:
laṅkāvirāmaṃ raṇaraṅgabhīmaṃ
rājīvanētraṃ raghuvaṃśamitram ।
kāruṇyamūrtiṃ karuṇāprapūrtiṃ
śrīrāmachandraṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 169
contentBlocks.translation: I take refuge in Sri Ramachandra — the one who rested in Lanka, terrible in the arena of battle, with lotus eyes, the friend of the Raghu dynasty, the form of compassion, the fulfilment of compassion. (169)

contentBlocks.original:
सुग्रीवमित्रं परमं पवित्रं
सीताकलत्रं नवहेमसूत्रम् ।
कारुण्यपात्रं शतपत्रनेत्रं
श्रीरामचन्द्रं शिरसा नमामि ॥ 170
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sugrīvamitraṃ paramaṃ pavitraṃ
sītākaḻatraṃ navahēmasūtram ।
kāruṇyapātraṃ śatapatranētraṃ
śrīrāmachandraṃ śirasā namāmi ॥ 170
contentBlocks.translation: I bow with my head to Sri Ramachandra — the friend of Sugriva, supremely pure, whose consort is Sita, of the new golden thread, the vessel of compassion, with eyes like a hundred-petalled lotus. (170)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीराघवेति रमणेति रघूद्वहेति
रामेति रावणहरेति रमाधवेति ।
साकेतनाथसुमुखेति च सुव्रतेति
वाणी सदा वदतु राम हरे हरेति ॥ 171
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāghavēti ramaṇēti raghūdvahēti
rāmēti rāvaṇaharēti ramādhavēti ।
sākētanāthasumukhēti cha suvratēti
vāṇī sadā vadatu rāma harē harēti ॥ 171
contentBlocks.translation: May my speech ever speak — "O Sri Raghava, O Ramana (the beautiful one), O lifter of the Raghu dynasty, O Rama, O destroyer of Ravana, O lord of Lakshmi, O lord of Saketa (Ayodhya), O beautiful-faced one, O one of noble vows" — Hari, Hari, Rama. (171)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामनामामृतमन्त्रबीजं
सञ्जीवनं चेन्मनसि प्रतिष्ठम् ।
हालाहलं वा प्रलयानलं वा
मृत्योर्मुखं वा वितथीकरोति ॥ 172
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmanāmāmṛtamantrabījaṃ
sañjīvanaṃ chēnmanasi pratiṣṭham ।
hālāhalaṃ vā praḻayānalaṃ vā
mṛtyōrmukhaṃ vā vitathīkarōti ॥ 172
contentBlocks.translation: If the seed of the nectar of the Sri Rama name mantra is established in the mind — it makes powerless the terrible poison, or the fire of dissolution, or even the face of death. (172)

contentBlocks.original:
किं योगशास्त्रैः किमशेषविद्या
किं यागगङ्गादिविशेषतीर्थैः ।
किं ब्रह्मचर्याश्रमसञ्चरेण
भक्तिर्नचेत्ते रघुवंशकीर्त्याम् ॥ 173
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kiṃ yōgaśāstraiḥ kimaśēṣavidyā
kiṃ yāgagaṅgādiviśēṣatīrthaiḥ ।
kiṃ brahmacharyāśramasañcharēṇa
bhaktirnachēttē raghuvaṃśakīrtyām ॥ 173
contentBlocks.translation: What use are the treatises on yoga? What use is the full range of learning? What use are sacrifices, the Ganga, or any special pilgrimage? What use is the path of brahmacharya and the hermitage — if there is no devotion to the glory of the Raghu dynasty? (173)

contentBlocks.original:
इदं शरीरं श्लथसन्धिजर्झरं
पतत्यवश्यं परिणामपेशलम् ।
किमौषथं पृच्छसि मूढ दुर्मते
निरामयं रामकथामृतं पिब ॥ 174
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ śarīraṃ ślathasandhijarjharaṃ
patatyavaśyaṃ pariṇāmapēśalam ।
kimauṣathaṃ pṛchChasi mūḍha durmatē
nirāmayaṃ rāmakathāmṛtaṃ piba ॥ 174
contentBlocks.translation: This body with its loosening joints decays and inevitably falls, ripened for its end. What medicine do you seek, O foolish and misguided one? Drink the disease-free nectar of Rama's story. (174)

contentBlocks.original:
हे रामभद्राश्रय हे कृपालो
हे भक्तलोकैकशरण्यमूर्ते ।
पुनीहि मां त्वच्चरणारविन्दं
जगत्पवित्रं शरणं ममाऽस्तु ॥ 175
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hē rāmabhadrāśraya hē kṛpāḻō
hē bhaktalōkaikaśaraṇyamūrtē ।
punīhi māṃ tvachcharaṇāravindaṃ
jagatpavitraṃ śaraṇaṃ mamā'stu ॥ 175
contentBlocks.translation: O my refuge, Ramabhadra, O compassionate one, O form that is the sole refuge of the devotees — purify me; may your lotus foot, the purifier of the universe, be my refuge. (175)

contentBlocks.original:
नीलाभ्रदेह निखिलेश जगन्निवास
राजीवनेत्र रमणीयगुणाभिराम ।
श्रीदाम दैत्यकुलमर्दन रामचन्द्र
त्वत्पादपद्ममनिशं कलयामि चित्ते ॥ 176
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nīlābhradēha nikhilēśa jagannivāsa
rājīvanētra ramaṇīyaguṇābhirāma ।
śrīdāma daityakulamardana rāmachandra
tvatpādapadmamaniśaṃ kalayāmi chittē ॥ 176
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate in my heart always the lotus feet of Sri Ramachandra — O body of the blue rain cloud, O lord of all, O universal dwelling, O lotus-eyed one, O one of beautiful and pleasing virtues, O giver of wealth (Sri), O crusher of the demon dynasty. (176)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामचन्द्र करुणाकर दीनबन्धो
सीतासमेत भरताग्रज राघवेश ।
पापार्तिभञ्जन भयातुरदीनबन्धो
पापाम्बुधौ पतितमुद्धर मामनाथम् ॥ 177
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmachandra karuṇākara dīnabandhō
sītāsamēta bharatāgraja rāghavēśa ।
pāpārtibhañjana bhayāturadīnabandhō
pāpāmbudhau patitamuddhara māmanātham ॥ 177
contentBlocks.translation: O Sri Ramachandra, compassionate one, friend of the lowly, O one accompanied by Sita, O elder brother of Bharata, O lord of the Raghus — O destroyer of the afflictions of sin, O friend of the fearful and wretched — lift me up, who have fallen into the ocean of sin, helpless and without refuge. (177)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्दीवरदलश्याम-मिन्दुकोटिनिभाननम् ।
कन्दर्पकोटिलावण्यं वन्देऽहं रघुनन्दनम् ॥ 175
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indīvaradaḻaśyāma-mindukōṭinibhānanam ।
kandarpakōṭilāvaṇyaṃ vandē'haṃ raghunandanam ॥ 175
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Raghunandana — dark blue as the petal of the blue lotus, whose face is like crores of moons, whose beauty equals crores of Kamadevs. (175)

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीबोधेन्द्रसरस्वती कृत श्रीरामकर्णामृतम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrībōdhēndrasarasvatī kṛta śrīrāmakarṇāmṛtam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Sri Rama Karnamrutam, composed by Sri Bodhendra Sarasvati.
